title: Research Index
blurb: This directory contains research documents analyzing design decisions, exploring architectural trade-offs, and documenting reasoning for the Swift Institute ecosystem.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.1.0, 2026-06-12, principal-reviewed): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. Memory.Foreign — the third ownership regime (memory we did not allocate, owned past the lending scope, released by provider callback, never deallocate; the mbuf ext_free / skb destructor / DPDK extbuf / io_uring provided-buffer / Apple Frame.customFinalizer pattern). Four verdicts: (1) SHAPE — single @frozen ~Copyable finalizer-only regime type in sibling pkg swift-memory-foreign-primitives: the owning envelope around the ecosystem's existing non-owning descriptor (Span.Raw.Mutable + plain finalizer closure + ~Copyable uniqueness; owner-release = closure capture, no enum, no Owner generic; ZERO Unsafe*/@Sendable/@unchecked on the public surface). NO Sendable conformance — terminal direction at birth [MEM-SEND-013]; isolation crossing is region-checked `consuming sending` ([MEM-SEND-010]/[MEM-SEND-012]; probe V5 both directions); the foreign storage column is non-Sendable by design and moves as a sending value. Non-trapping RAII deinit; @unsafe adopting init whose unsafety is the OWNERSHIP CLAIM (exclusivity, lifetime validity, finalizer-actually-releases), not parameter types; take() via Optional-finalizer/guarded-deinit (discard-wall discovery: `discard self` requires trivially-destroyed stored props on 6.3.2 — Completion.Entry shape instead); mutable-exclusive + element-alignment adoption contract (assumingMemoryBound UB on misaligned base; alignment is the adopter's obligation; Span.Raw-fed Foreign is byte-element in practice). (2) TAXONOMY — Foreign = owning envelope for provider-released memory; Map/Shared = kernel-mediated mapping vocabulary + non-owning descriptors; different cuts that compose (owning mapping = Foreign over Map verbs); no retrofit; mmap dropped; in-scope motivation narrowed to the RECEIVE path (send pinning + retransmission wait on the read-only column + sharing model, Residual #3/#4). (3) MEMORY.PROTOCOL VERDICT: NO new protocol — Memory.Region IS the memory-tier protocol (base+capacity only, written non-collapse invariant, consulted ONCE at construction, zero hot-path dispatch); release semantics are regime identity and stay out of any protocol; Span.Protocol is a different cut (already lifted namespace-neutral); additive follow-ons only (Memory.Region seam laws in test support; Apple-HarnessProtocols-style substrate swapping needs ZERO new protocols). (4) TIMING — design adopted now; package creation gated on first consumer arc (sockets 2B+/proactor) AND the tower publication gate; buffer-tier where-clause generalization ROUTED to the tower program with a PRE-STAGED one-op relaxation experiment spec (relax e.g. Buffer.Ring.pushBack to the Store.Ledgered bound on a branch; instantiate heap + Inline + Foreign columns). EMPIRICAL (swift-buffer-ring-primitives/Experiments/foreign-region-tower-instantiation, Swift 6.3.2, 2026-06-12): payoff thesis PARTIAL — Storage tier CONFIRMED zero-change (Memory.Region convenience init; 4-op seam; oracle-then-finalizer exactly-once); Buffer tier REFUTED (every public Ring entry point same-type-pinned to the heap column, diagnostics captured; the pins fence out ALL non-heap regions incl. shipping Memory.Inline = pre-existing tower-generality gap); V3 = interaction-pattern evidence ONLY (hand-replay over Store.Ledgered, identical output from heap + foreign columns; does NOT compile the pinned bodies — the one-op relaxation compile is the definitive receipt); V5 sending target under .strictMemorySafety() + ecosystem features: positive (Sendable-capture finalizer crosses as consuming sending, finalizer exactly-once in the sink's isolation) AND negative (MainActor-entangled finalizer rejected AT THE SEND SITE: "sending 'foreign' risks causing data races [#SendingRisksDataRace]") CONFIRMED; spelling discovery: `sending` alone does not specify ownership for ~Copyable params — `consuming sending`. CONTEXTUALIZATION CORRECTED (R3): uniqueness suffices for 1:1 buffer-to-packet custody (io_uring rings = the named first consumer) but surveyed refcounts ALSO enable N references to one region (DPDK extbuf carving, skb_clone retransmission) — single-owner ~Copyable cannot represent N packets windowing one region; sharing rebuilt at a higher tier or forfeited to copies (named open question, packet-currency arc). Prior art primary-source verified per [RES-020] (FreeBSD m_extadd/m_ext_free_t; Linux skb destructor + ubuf_info ops->complete; DPDK rte_pktmbuf_attach_extbuf + RTE_MBUF_F_EXTERNAL, refcnt co-located in ext shared info; io_uring PBUF_RING re-provide custody window; SwiftNIO 2.96.0 @_spi(CustomByteBufferAllocator) takingOwnershipOf = allocator-vtable shape under which per-buffer finalizers stay inexpressible — the instructive negative; SE-0447/SE-0446 Implemented 6.2, lifetime annotations = unmerged PR#2305; Apple Frame's plain-closure finalizer MATCHES the institute posture — their gap is unchecked transfers, ours are compiler-checked region sends). Corrects 3 stale brief premises. Receive-path provenance lean: everything-is-Foreign (monomorphic column), decided finally at the packet-currency arc. NO package/namespace created this arc.

Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. Supersedes the 2026-06-12 in-conversation Memory.Foreign brief. documents.displayName: Occupancy Lives in the Leaf — the Buffer-Tower Placement Law
documents.topic: DECISION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-07, ratified by the principal): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. THE PLACEMENT LAW — liveness tracking and element teardown live in a single-allocation LEAF, never in the Buffer. Dense leaves track liveness as a contiguous range-ledger (Store.Initialization/Memory.Tracked); sparse leaves track it per-slot (bitmap/generation/free-list) SoA within the one allocation. A Buffer NEVER carries an element-teardown deinit — it is a thin generic over a leaf carrying only the access discipline (count/cursor/indices/links); Storage.Contiguous<Memory.X> lifts dense and sparse leaves uniformly. There is NO Storage.Sparse wrapper, NO .Inline/.Small Buffer types (the occupancy carve-outs dissolve; .Bounded is a separate lawful capacity axis, retained), and NO raw allocator as a Buffer backing. Copyability flows from the leaf: a move-only @_rawLayout inline leaf yields a move-only buffer instance; a class-backed heap leaf yields a conditionally-Copyable one — from ONE generic Buffer type. Supersedes the conditional-deinit note's 'permanent carve-out / converged equilibrium' conclusion (the Wall-1 PROOF stands; the conclusion analyzed the wrong architecture — occupancy held in the buffer forcing a buffer deinit). Locates the bug: Buffer.Slab.Header.swift:27 bitmap in the buffer header. The existence proof for the sparse case already ships: Storage.Arena (single-alloc sparse leaf, Backing-class teardown). The one honest residual: a bit-dense INLINE sparse leaf has a deinit => move-only, so the only thing unattainable is bit-density+value-semantics+inline simultaneously (one corner's copyability, NOT a type explosion). Wall-2/swift#86652 unchanged ([MEM-SAFE-027]). Corrects [DS-023], [MEM-COPY-016].
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0 (2026-06-07), ratified by the principal. Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Implementation bottom-to-top on Swift 6.3.2: Store.Sparse.Protocol leaves, reshape Buffer.{Slab,Linked,Arena} to thin generics, dissolve .Inline/.Small types + PoolStorage/NodeStorage, demote raw Memory.Pool/Arena to explicit-only. Index entry backfilled 2026-06-08 (the doc shipped 2026-06-07 but was not registered).

documents.displayName: Occupancy Encoding 5 — Cross-Language Prior Art and the Vacuity Verdict
documents.topic: DECISION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-08): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Angle 5 of the shared occupancy-trilemma investigation (can an occupancy-bearing container achieve ALL of: (A) ONE Store.Protocol no-refinement; (B) bit-density <=1 bit/slot; (C) value-semantics / conditional Copyable with .Inline/.Small carve-out TYPES dissolved into one generic Buffer<S>; (D) self-cleaning teardown no buffer deinit / SE-0427 Wall-1 avoided; (E) maximal decompose+compose). Two deliverables the prior corpus lacks ([RES-019]-verified absent): a cross-language occupancy-ENCODING survey mapped to A-E + ~Copyable portability, and the VACUITY CENSUS of the tower's actual sparse consumers. 6.3.2 REPRO (mandate gate; TOOLCHAINS=org.swift.632202605101a = Swift 6.3.2, [Verified 2026-06-08], /tmp/occ-vacuity/): the bit-dense inline sparse leaf (@_rawLayout + inline UInt64 bitmap + deinit walking it) COMPILES but is unconditionally ~Copyable, blocked DOUBLY -- (1) copyable_illegal_deinit (SE-0427) AND (2) @_rawLayout non-Copyable storage poison; either alone forces move-only. Achievable corners S2/S5/assembled/tombstone all compile; the Element? tombstone is Copyable but spends bit-density (discriminator per slot). PRIOR-ART SURVEY (primary sources, [Verified 2026-06-08]): Rust is the LINCHPIN -- slotmap SlotMap/HopSlotMap HISTORICALLY required V:Copy 'due to current stable Rust restrictions' (dense (value,version) parity-occupancy slots = the SAME wall), RELAXED by DenseSlotMap (separate dense Vec, SoA = the Storage.Arena move) + generational-arena/thunderdome Option<T> tombstones; deprecated Slottable note 'slotmap now supports all types on stable' = lifted by LAYOUT change not a language feature. slab=enum Entry{Occupied(T),Vacant(usize)}. hashbrown SwissTable control-byte plane (EMPTY/DELETED/7-bit h2, groups of 16, SIMD) = SoA occupancy in the same alloc as slots = Storage.Split shape vindicated at scale (byte-dense by deliberate speed choice). C++ std::optional (separate bool, NO niche opt, trivial dtor iff T trivially-destructible) + P0848R3 conditionally-trivial dtors = the ONE all-three-on-inline model, NON-PORTABLE (decouples dtor from copyability, the axes Swift couples). EnTT dense+sparse arrays; Zig MultiArrayList (no copyability concept -> SoA by default = degenerate confirmation); plf::colony/std::hive skipfield (jump-counts, not bit-dense). SYNTHESIS: every encoding achieving B+C+inline simultaneously is either C++-non-portable or tombstone-spends-B; NO copyability-coupled language gives bit-dense+value-sem+inline together; Rust HAD dense-inline-Copy and REMOVED it via SoA/tombstones. The excluded corner is the UNIVERSAL excluded corner ([RES-021]: restriction is the cross-language norm; deliberate equilibrium not gap). VACUITY CENSUS (grep all 3 orgs, file:line-verified, 2026-06-08): every REAL sparse consumer is HEAP-backed -- Dictionary=Buffer<Storage<Key>.Contiguous<Memory.Heap>>.Slab (Dictionary.swift:99-101); Tree.N/Keyed/Unbounded=Buffer<Storage<Node>.Arena>.Arena (Tree.N.swift:72); Async.Timer.Wheel=...Arena.Bounded (heap+capacity axis); List.Linked/Queue.Linked=...Contiguous<Memory.Heap>>.Linked<N> (List.Linked.swift:78). ZERO non-owning consumers instantiate ANY inline-sparse variant (Slab.Static/Buffer.Slab.Inline/.Small/Buffer.Arena.Inline/Linked.Inline) anywhere in primitives+foundations(4614 files)+standards -- only self-references in the storage-arena package's own .Inline decl files. VERDICT: VACUOUS=YES -- no real consumer hits the excluded corner (bit-dense+value-sem+inline+spare-bit-less element); the heap sparse leaf (Storage.Arena pattern) delivers ALL of A-E simultaneously; the maximal-achievable point IS 'all of A-E' for EVERY real consumer. The trilemma is real (teeth reproduced on 6.3.2; universal cross-language exclusion) but is a statement about an EMPTY corner; its only cost (move-only-ness of a bit-dense inline sparse LEAF) is paid by no current consumer and, if ever needed, paid as ONE move-only leaf (not a family, already modeled by Memory.Inline) with tombstone as the C-for-B swap. Corroborates+extends occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf.md (2026-06-07 DECISION) from two independent directions (cross-language + vacuity); supplies the missing empirical backing for the decomposition-layer-placement-package-map.md §C.3 hard-floor SUPERSESSION (the retained Buffer.{Slab,Linked,Arena}.{Inline,Small} forced-keeps have ZERO inhabitants). RESEARCH ONLY -- no tower edits; introduces no new disposition.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-06): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Can a conditionally-Copyable generic struct carry a deinit that runs ONLY for its ~Copyable instantiations (the missing capability that would let the MSB tower's sparse+inline+move-only buffers go pure-generic with no concrete .Inline variant)? EMPIRICAL VERDICT (8 spikes S1-S8 + all deinit experimental flags, on Swift 6.3.2 AND 6.4-dev, [Verified 2026-06-06]): NOT expressible now -- every spelling fails with `copyable_illegal_deinit` ('deinitializer cannot be declared in generic struct that conforms to Copyable'); `deinit where` is not grammar (S3); deinit-in-extension independently forbidden (S4); NO experimental flag unlocks it; no struct-only escape (S8 -- only a heap CLASS relocates a custom deinit while preserving conditional Copyable, which defeats inline storage). TWO DISTINCT WALLS kept separate: Wall 1 = Sema (this note) `copyable_illegal_deinit` at DiagnosticsSema.def:8390, emitted unconditionally at TypeCheckInvertible.cpp:228-233, added by commit a4e9919f290 (Kavon Farvardin 2023-11-06); BY DESIGN per OwnershipManifesto.md:1473-1478 ('does not conform to Copyable (even conditionally) may define a deinit') and SE-0427 § Conformance to Copyable ('A conditional Copyable conformance is not permitted if the struct or enum declares a deinit. Deterministic destruction requires the type to be unconditionally noncopyable.' -- author-verified against raw source). Wall 2 = IRGen swiftlang/swift#86652 (OPEN, filed coenttb 2026-01-20, no upstream PR) -- even the unconditionally-~Copyable buffer's deinit is SKIPPED cross-package when @_rawLayout storage is cross-module-composed; a bounded codegen bug, principal's fork branches already attacking it. FUNDAMENTAL-vs-UNIMPLEMENTED is two-level: the LAW deinit=>(unconditionally)~Copyable is fundamental (soundness; double-destroy), but the FEATURE conditional-deinit RESPECTS that law (deinit only for non-duplicable ~Copyable instantiations) and is SOUND-in-principle but deliberately excluded by SE-0427's simpler whole-type model -- an Evolution-scale generalization, not a bug. FORMAL [RES-024]: Swift ALREADY has conditional AUTOMATIC field teardown (S7 runs: move-only field deinit fires, Copyable instantiation copies); the gap is conditional CUSTOM (sparse-bitmap) teardown only. PRIOR ART [RES-021] (parallel-subagent primary-source verified): Rust is the LINCHPIN and REFUTES 'Swift is behind' -- IDENTICAL restriction (E0184 Drop=>!Copy; E0367 forbids conditional Drop impls), IDENTICAL escapes (hoist-bound / wrapper), only-conditional-destruction is automatic drop-glue (= S7); custom sparse cleanup uses ManuallyDrop + inner non-Copy type (= Box-relocation). C++ is the lone exception (P0848R3 conditionally-trivial dtors; optional/variant) ONLY because its destructor is decoupled from copyability (rule-of-three deprecates, not deletes) -- not portable to Swift's deinit-IS-the-copyability-switch model. The [Pitch] Box thread (Alejandro Alonso 2026-01-07) resolves the IDENTICAL shape by making Box unconditionally ~Copyable -- the institute's .Inline hard-floor = exactly Apple's own answer. BEST MOVE (layered): (1) NOW land #86652 (Wall 2) implement-in-fork + upstream PR -- the real bounded win, makes the inline hard-floor leak-free cross-package (cleave-7 Phase-1); (2) KEEP the .Inline unconditional-~Copyable hard-floor as a CONVERGED design equilibrium (cleave-7 §C.3), not workaround debt; (3) DEFER the conditional-deinit Swift Evolution pitch (sketch held, PITCH-PROC-004) -- do NOT fork-implement a language feature as a shipping path; revisit only with more motivating cases. Confidence: Wall-2 fix HIGH; evergreen-arrives-without-action LOW; pitch-acceptance LOW-MEDIUM (must overturn SE-0427 + Box precedent + McCall's deinit-linear-feature skepticism). Internal-first [RES-019]: extends buffer-arena-conditional-copyable.md (IMPLEMENTED) which solved the HEAP case via Storage-class relocation and flagged Inline as needing 'Swift language evolution' -- this note names that evolution precisely.

documents.displayName: Occupancy Encoding (1) — ADT Cell Layout and the Extra-Inhabitant Frontier
documents.topic: DECISION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-08): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Angle 1 of the occupancy-encoding program — attacks (A)–(E) via the element CELL and its bit-level layout. The cell Slot<E> = Optional<E> (stdlib Optional, used directly) is the MAXIMAL ADT-layer point: achieves (A) one neutral Store.Protocol no-sparse-refinement (occupancy in-band cell==nil; verified conforming with SuppressedAssociatedTypes, ~Copyable element, no deinit), (C) value-semantics/conditional-Copyable, (D) self-cleaning no-deinit (no SE-0427 Wall-1), (E) maximal decomposition — ALL UNCONDITIONALLY for every E; and (B) <=1-bit (0 marginal bit) density IFF E exports >=1 extra-inhabitant (niche). HEADLINE FINDING: stdlib Optional is ALREADY ~Copyable/~Escapable-aware with conditional Copyable/BitwiseCopyable/Sendable/ExpressibleByNilLiteral (primary-source: arm64-apple-macos.swiftinterface:16176-16188) — so the cell delivers (C) for ~Copyable resource elements with NO custom type; end-to-end teardown verified (dealloc fires on .none-reassign and consume). A hand-rolled ~Copyable Slot enum reproduces stdlib Optional EXACTLY (layout + conditional-Copyable) — therefore redundant; recommendation is to use Optional directly. Niche discovery is AUTOMATIC + derivable FOR FREE — survives struct nesting (Wrap2{Wrap1{Ref}}?=8) and reuses any aggregate field's spare (struct{Ref,Bool}?=9 free) — so NO BitPackable protocol is needed in the free case. RESIDUAL (xi(E)=0: full-domain Int/UInt8/UInt64, padding-free PODs): the cell CANNOT be bit-dense within Optional's totality — tag rounds to a full word (Int?=16B stride = 64 bit/slot, measured 64x worse than the bitmap's 1 bit/slot). Recovery is NOT an ADT move: either leave the cell for the leaf bit-plane (AX-L bitmap), or MANUAL bit-stealing (DenseSlot+SpareBearing.makeVacant() reserving a sentinel) = 0 bit/slot at the cost of totality + a consumer invariant. ~Copyable ownership wrinkle: spare-bit vacancy MUST be vended as an OWNED value (static func makeVacant()->Self), not a borrowed {get} property (getter-form fails: 'borrowed and cannot be consumed'). Full element-class FREE/TAG taxonomy measured (class/enum/Bool/ptr/ObjectIdentifier/niche-aggregate=FREE; UInt8/Pair2/UInt64/Int=TAG). VERDICT: the cell ADT reaches its maximal point with NO new language feature; the one residual it cannot dense-encode (xi=0) is exactly angle-4's cell-14, a PLACEMENT (not cell-ADT) limitation collapsed only by a conditional deinit in combination with inline+value-semantics. EXTENDS occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf.md (AX-L) + occupancy-encoding-4-placement-proof.md (§I.4 niche cost = AX-4; tables agree exactly on 6.3.2) + conditional-deinit note (AX-1/S7); does NOT contradict. Promotion candidates: [DS-023] (quantify tombstone density 0/1/64 bit/slot by xi), [MEM-COPY-016] (Optional is ~Copyable-aware), new [MEM-OWN-*] (owned-vacancy rule).

Per [RES-023]/[RES-024] every load-bearing layout/diagnostic/behavioral claim compiled & run on Apple Swift 6.3.2 (swift-6.3.2-RELEASE, TOOLCHAINS=org.swift.632202605101a) [Verified: 2026-06-08]: MemoryLayout FREE/TAG taxonomy; Optional ~Copyable-awareness end-to-end teardown + interface citation (16176-16188); custom-Slot==Optional layout parity + conditional-Copyable enforcement; density 0/1/64 bit/slot; recursive niche through nesting/aggregates; manual DenseSlot 0-bit + owned-vacancy ~Copyable wrinkle; neutral-protocol conformance (A). documents.displayName: Occupancy Encoding (3) — The Swift 6.3.2 Mechanism Frontier (incl. Macros)
documents.topic: DECISION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-08): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Angle 3 of the multi-angle occupancy-trilemma study — THE SWIFT 6.3.2 MECHANISM FRONTIER incl. MACROS (the mechanism-axis companion to occupancy-encoding-4's information/satisfiability proof). Maps every Swift 6.3.2 mechanism against {compiles? moves the excluded cell?} via new N1-N14 + M0-M3 spikes (all 6.3.2-verified, TOOLCHAINS=org.swift.632202605101a = swift-6.3.2-RELEASE, /tmp/occ3-mechanisms/; S1-S8 baseline re-verified). KEY RESULTS: (1) the inline corner is OVER-DETERMINED — move-only for TWO independent reasons: Wall-1/SE-0427 (deinit, S1) AND @_rawLayout itself ('error: type with @_rawLayout cannot be copied and must be declared ~Copyable', N4, with NO deinit) — so [DS-002]'s @_rawLayout clause is a SECOND obstruction, not a restatement; collapsing the pointer-stable inline corner needs conditional-deinit PLUS conditional-@_rawLayout, two coordinated features not one. (2) InlineArray (SE-0453) IS the conditional-Copyable inline leaf @_rawLayout cannot be: conditionally Copyable + automatic per-element teardown (NO user deinit, so Wall-1 never applies) + inline + a ~Copyable-element _read/_modify seam, all RUN on 6.3.2 (N5/N7/N13) — empirically realizing doc-4's cell-10 (InlineArray+count) and cell-13 (niche tombstone) optima. Tombstone InlineArray<n,T?> gives sparse (B)+(C)+(D)+inline SIMULTANEOUSLY when the element has a niche (class/pointer/Bool/enum/padded-struct FREE; Int/UInt8 COST 2x — N9 density survey matches doc-4 Thm I.3 independently). The residual is the NO-NICHE scalar sub-case only. (3) RECONCILES storage-small-substrate.md v1.0.1: that note's InlineArray-route withdrawal was about the POINTER-STABLE Store.Protocol seam (raw-pointer Offset arithmetic, stable element pointer); InlineArray serves the VALUE-SEMANTICS seam (whole-value _read/_modify or enum-arm unpack/repack, N14c) — complementary, not contradictory. The residual is a SEAM-SHAPE trade as well as a density trade (a fourth facet). (4) MACROS (the central angle): a WORKING @SparseLeaf member+extension macro synthesizes a complete conditionally-Copyable tombstone sparse leaf — storage, seam, AND the conditional-Copyable conformance — from a one-line annotation, built+run on 6.3.2 (/tmp/occ3-mechanisms/SparseMacro/). BUT macros CANNOT dissolve the carve-out: two same-named copyability modes = 'invalid redeclaration' (M0); NO type-level predicate on element layout/spare-bits to branch on (M3, 'expected , separator'); a macro is TRANSPARENT to the copyability predicate (formal: Copyable(mu(A)) <=> Copyable(expand(mu,A)) — the expanded source hits AX-1/S1 and N4 like any source); the conditional deinit is an IRGen per-instantiation decision a source->source function structurally cannot make. The most a macro emits is TWO DISTINCTLY-NAMED types (M2) — RE-MANUFACTURING the carve-out, not dissolving it. DECISIVE drawback: a macro-synthesized leaf is Embedded-BARRED from L1 ([PRIM-FOUND-002]) where the occupancy lives — disqualifying for the leaf tier regardless of debuggability/~Copyable-audit costs. discard/~Escapable/@_specialize/ownership-specifiers/suppressed-protocols all FAIL to move cell 14 (N10/N11/N12). VERDICT: maximal achievable point = ONE generic Buffer<S:Store.Protocol> over a small leaf lattice where the leaf is CHOSEN (not macro-synthesized) per (backing x occupancy x seam-shape x element-niche); all of A-E hold for EVERY config except the intersection {no-niche scalar (N9) x inline (N4+N1) x pointer-stable seam (N14)} — drop ANY ONE and it dissolves (niche->tombstone; heap->class; value-seam->InlineArray). The boundary-lifting feature is doc-4's conditional deinit, refined here: the pointer-stable inline corner needs conditional-deinit AND conditional-@_rawLayout. EXTENDS occupancy-encoding-4 (mechanism-axis dual, sharpens Thm III.3), occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf (a leaf-tier macro would REINTRODUCE the buffer-dissolved carve-out, M2), conditional-deinit-... (S1-S8 consumed as axioms), storage-small-substrate (reconciled), macro-composition-architecture (the macro-transparency formal note). RESEARCH ONLY — no tower edits; proposes [DS-002]/[MEM-COPY-016] cross-reference promotions, no rule-text change.

documents.displayName: Occupancy Encoding (4) — Placement Proof and Information-Theoretic Lower Bound
documents.topic: DECISION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-08): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Makes the buffer tower a THEOREM — proves the exact frontier of an occupancy-bearing container w.r.t. (A) one Store.Protocol no-refinement, (B) bit-density <=1 bit/slot, (C) value-semantics/conditional-Copyable in one generic Buffer<S>, (D) self-cleaning teardown no-buffer-deinit (SE-0427 Wall-1 avoided), (E) maximal decomposition. EXTENDS occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf.md (placement law = AX-4) and consumes conditional-deinit-conditionally-copyable-generics.md's SE-0427 Wall-1 proof + S1-S8 matrix as AXIOMS (S1+S2+S5+S7 re-reproduced on Swift 6.3.2). PART I INFORMATION FLOOR (proved + 6.3.2-measured via MemoryLayout): contiguous-prefix liveness floor = Theta(log N), achieved EXACTLY by Store.Initialization range-ledger (.empty/.one/.two); arbitrary liveness floor = N bits, achieved EXACTLY by the bitmap (info-optimal); per-element Optional tag costs 0 MARGINAL bits IFF the element exports >=1 extra-inhabitant (niche-fill, where it UNIQUELY beats the bitmap by spending zero dedicated bits), else >=8 bit/slot (alignment-rounded) — measured: class/Bool/enum/pointer=0 marginal, UInt8=+1B, Int=+8B/64-bit-slot. (B) honored by ledger/bitmap always, by tag ONLY under a niche. PART II PLACEMENT LATTICE (proved): liveness may live (i) in the element cell (E_tag, AUTOMATIC Optional destructor, NO custom deinit), (ii) separate plane in the leaf, (iii) in the buffer (retired by AX-4). (i)=>tag-or-spare-bit; (ii)/(iii)=>custom deinit, which on a CLASS stays conditional-Copyable (AX-2 class-exemption) but on an inline @_rawLayout STRUCT is forced UNCONDITIONALLY ~Copyable by SE-0427 Wall-1 (AX-1, copyable_illegal_deinit S1-reproduced on 6.3.2). PART III SATISFIABILITY MATRIX (proved): over the 16-cell grid (backing{heap,inline} x occupancy{dense,sparse} x want-VS{y,n} x spare-bit{y,n}), (A)+(D)+(E) hold UNCONDITIONALLY; the UNIQUE excluded cell is #14 {inline x sparse x want-value-semantics x no-spare-bit} where (B) and (C) are PROVABLY mutually exclusive (Thm II.5: no-niche forbids cheap tag => side-plane mandatory; inline side-plane => struct deinit => AX-1 => no (C)). Pareto-optimal encoding tabulated per cell: range-ledger (dense), bitmap/tokens (sparse, Storage.Arena=cell-6 shipped existence proof), niche-tag where a spare bit exists (cells 5/7/13/15, dominates bitmap), 'InlineArray+count' for dense-inline-VS (cell 10 — therefore NOT excluded; a prefix needs no teardown oracle). Thm III.2: the excluded region is NOT vacuous — inhabited by Int/Double/full-domain-integers/padding-free-structs (xi=0, natural class). Thm III.3 (collapse): EXACTLY ONE language feature collapses cell 14 — a CONDITIONAL deinit (SE-0427's deliberately-excluded generalization, sound-in-principle, held as PITCH-0003, not fork-implementable); no weaker mechanism suffices (tag needs a type-layout niche unavailable by hypothesis; class needs a heap alloc contradicting inline). The tower's default never ENTERS cell 14 (move-only inline leaf yields a move-only buffer INSTANCE from the ONE generic type; the carve-out TYPE dissolves, (E) holds). One-sentence frontier: (A)+(D)+(E) free everywhere; (B)+(C) coexist everywhere EXCEPT {inline x sparse x no-niche}, collapsed by and only by a conditional deinit.

Per [RES-023]/[RES-024] every load-bearing layout/diagnostic claim verified on Apple Swift 6.3.2 (swift-6.3.2-RELEASE, TOOLCHAINS=org.swift.632202605101a) [Verified: 2026-06-08]: S1 wall repro (copyable_illegal_deinit); S2+S5+S7 assembly compiles+runs; MemoryLayout extra-inhabitant niche table; bitmap-vs-tombstone density (1.0 vs 64 vs 0 marginal bit/slot). documents.displayName: Memory, Byte, and Bit — Domain Orthogonality and Bridge Decomposition
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-03): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Composes with the APPROVED cross-layer-capability-protocol-model.md (CLCPM, Tier 3 capability normal form: minimal cores + orthogonal cross-cutting concerns, compose-don't-refine) and the IMPLEMENTED binary-byte-namespace-domain-foundations.md (Bit->Byte->Binary representation axis; omits Memory). Supplies the orthogonal Memory (location/layout) axis. CRITICAL ASYMMETRY (Q1): memory<->bit is ESSENTIAL+intrinsic but UNDER-TYPED -- Memory.Shift/Alignment is a COUNT OF BIT POSITIONS; fix = type it onto Bit.Index.Count (=Tagged<Bit,Cardinal>, exists at swift-bit-index-primitives Bit.Index.swift:26, used Bit.Pack.Location.swift:94) and repoint the dep bit-primitives->bit-index-primitives; rawValue:UInt8 (Memory.Shift.swift:34) is the legacy (principal: 'the UInt8 part is vestigial, Bit is the intended one'). memory<->byte is NOT a real tie. KEY FINDING [Verified 2026-06-03]: the only byte edge is the span-vending protocol __Memory_Contiguous_Borrowed_Protocol, whose own header (:20-35) shows it ALREADY has THREE cross-domain conformers -- Memory.Contiguous.Borrowed, Byte.Borrowed, Binary.Borrowed -- so naming it 'Memory.Contiguous.Borrowed.Protocol' is a MISNOMER and IS the byte/binary->memory coupling; and owned vs borrowed CANNOT be one protocol (witness-table contract for var span differs across lifetime regimes: owned borrows self+_overrideLifetime, borrowed is ~Escapable). RECOMMENDATION (Option C, supersedes the earlier memory-byte bridge which only RELOCATED the conformance and ignored binary): (1) LIFT span into a namespace-neutral swift-span-primitives capability -- Span.Protocol (owned, @_lifetime(borrow self)) + Span.Borrowed.Protocol (~Escapable, @_lifetime(copy self)) + Span.Mutable.Protocol (refine); Memory.Contiguous/Byte.Borrowed/Binary.Borrowed each conform; NO memory<->byte/binary edge survives; retires the __-mangled workaround; cursor generalizes to where DomainTag.Borrowed: Span.Borrowed.Protocol. (2) BIT -> type Memory.Shift onto Bit.Index.Count, repoint to bit-index-primitives. (3) REJECT memory-bit bridge (pool already owns the only memory(x)bit join, Bit.Vector bitmap, Memory.Pool.swift:101); REJECT Memory.Protocol (vacuous marker, no op once span lifts); do NOT invent Contiguous.Run (reuse existing swift-interval/range-primitives). HOLD Option E (Contiguous-topology split) as separate/optional -- not needed to dissolve the byte coupling. Compose-don't-precompose (M: Memory.* & Span.Mutable.Protocol, never nested Memory.Contiguous.Mutable.Protocol per API-NAME-002). [RES-018] WAIVED per principal. Refines CLCPM's 'EXTEND Memory.Contiguous, no change' disposition (CLCPM already has Storage PROVIDE span, anticipating this) -> must fold back as a CLCPM 3.4 amendment. PRIOR ART: layout universally decoupled from a byte value-type (Rust core/mem+alloc, Swift MemoryLayout, Reynolds Heap=Address|->Value); 'vends a contiguous view' is a CAPABILITY everywhere (Rust AsRef/Deref, C++ std::span/contiguous_range, Swift ContiguousBytes), never namespace-tied -> warrant for Span.Protocol; bit-axis typing is stricter-than-prior-art (deliberate). Bottom-up; consumers refactor. Research only; class-(c); principal decides. Caveat: swift-memory-primitives--msb checkout active 2026-06-03 13:23.

Per [RES-023] verified on disk at file:line 2026-06-03: __Memory_Contiguous_Borrowed_Protocol.swift:20-56 (3 cross-domain conformers + the structural owned/borrowed lifetime split rationale), Memory.ContiguousProtocol.swift:77, Memory.Shift.swift:34, Bit.Index.swift:26 + Bit.Pack.Location.swift:94, Memory.Pool.swift:101, existence of swift-interval/range-primitives (so Contiguous.Run must NOT be invented), non-existence of swift-span-primitives. EVOLUTION: v-draft-1 mislabeled bit 'vestigial' (corrected by principal -> essential+under-typed); v-draft-2 recommended a memory-byte BRIDGE (superseded by principal's Span-decomposition steer -> the span LIFT dissolves the coupling at the root AND fixes binary, strictly better than relocating). A suspect subagent claim (Memory.Address struct snippet contradicting the verified typealias) was NOT carried. [RES-018] explicitly waived by principal for the new Span.(Mutable.)Protocol primitives. Open for principal: Byte.Borrowed:Span.Borrowed.Protocol placement (byte gains span dep vs tiny bridge), swift-span-primitives scope/naming + Raw/Output now-or-later, bit exponent type (Bit.Index.Count vs dedicated Bit.Shift), Option E defer, CLCPM-amendment ownership, --msb coordination, span-primitives+typed-Shift 0-witness spike.

documents.displayName: Buffer-Namespace Membership: Occupancy Disciplines vs Raw Byte Regions
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-02): Tier 2 cross-layer. GAP-O of derive-for-free-capability-composition.md: which Buffer.* members are genuine LOGICAL occupancy disciplines (count distinct from capacity, over a physical storage) vs raw byte regions / raw slot regions that belong at the PHYSICAL layer (Memory or Storage). Governed by principal directives (2026-06-02): (1) 'Buffer should be the logical layer over physical storage primitives'; (2) 'cannot ignore the storage primitives packages'. CLASSIFICATION TEST: a Buffer member tracks occupancy (count) distinct from allocation (capacity); a raw region has count == capacity; raw slots have no logical count. INVENTORY (18 Buffer.Protocol conformers + Slots, verified on disk file:line 2026-06-02): the 5 families Linear/Ring/Slab/Arena/Linked all track count != capacity (occupancy stored/computed separately, each composing a Storage.* primitive) -> STAY. THREE MISCLASSIFIED: Buffer.Aligned (count == allocated bytes, no separate capacity; already conforms Memory.Contiguous.Protocol; allocates aligned via UnsafeMutableRawPointer) -> RELOCATE to Memory layer as Memory.Aligned (sibling of Memory.Contiguous/Buffer; Memory.Contiguous is adopt-only so aligned-allocation is a genuine gap, [DS-020] satisfied); Buffer.Unbounded (count and capacity are byte-identical expressions _storage.count.retag; occupancy delegated to Binary.Cursor.writerIndex; 0 consumers) -> RELOCATE to Memory (Memory.Aligned.Resizable / Memory.Unbounded); Buffer.Slots (non-conformer; no count; 'same contract as Storage.Split'; backed by Storage.Split; Header 'trivial -- just capacity. Unlike Linear (count)' redundant with Storage.Split's own Header(capacity)) -> RELOCATE to Storage layer (fold into / beside Storage.Split; sole consumer Hash.Table). BLAST RADIUS: tiny -- Unbounded 0 dependents, Aligned 1 (Unbounded), Slots 1 (Hash.Table->Dictionary/Set). [RES-023] CORRECTION: brief's 'Binary.Cursor uses Unbounded' REFUTED on disk -- Binary.Cursor is generic over Memory.Contiguous.Protocol, not Buffer.Unbounded (reinforces Memory relocation). SEQUENCING: GAP-O first (makes Buffer.Protocol.count unambiguously occupancy, removes the 2 vacuous-fullness byte buffers) -> then GAP-N revivable (the {capacity,isFull,availableCapacity} surface becomes coherent). Identity-driven not adoption-driven ([RES-029]/[ARCH-LAYER-006/008]). Extends + governed by the APPROVED cross-layer-capability-protocol-model (logical Buffer.Protocol vs physical Memory.Contiguous.Protocol/Storage.Protocol) and storage-buffer-abstraction-analysis (Aligned/Unbounded historically a SEPARATE Tier-16 byte-buffer package, excluded from the 6-discipline inventory). DESIGN/RATIONALE ONLY -- no package edits; class-(c), principal decides.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-02): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. The institute's versioning + release strategy and the concrete bottom-up path from today's state (all deps URL-form, ~99% branch:"main" vs ~1% from:, 0 path; ~400 mostly-private layered packages; INVERTED frontier where L2 specs are public+tagged atop a mostly-private post-modularization L1) to genuine outsider-buildable releases. RECOMMENDS independent per-package semver (Model B), NOT a synchronized universal version -- dispositive evidence: ~68 independent standards tag lines already exist (rfc-3986@0.3.6 ... rfc-5322@0.7.4), lockstep is unreachable without regressing real releases, SwiftPM has no lockstep primitive. First tag 0.1.0 (not 0.0.1): empirically verified SwiftPM 0.x semantics (Swift 6.3.2) -- from:/upToNextMajor on 0.x spans the ENTIRE 0.x line to <1.0.0 (NOT a Cargo/npm 0.x caret), and .upToNextMinor(from:0.0.1) is a degenerate empty band; disciplined pre-1.0 pin is .upToNextMinor(from:0.Y.0). Two bottom-up passes: visibility back-fill (private->public on rolling branch:"main", gated by clean-room no-token/no-mirror resolve) THEN per-package tagging campaign with atomic branch:"main"->from: switch -- one-form-per-package invariant (branch-req silently overrides version-req for the same package; verified Swift 6.3.2). Keep existing standards tags; continue each line (don't reset). Reframes principal's 'universal first tag' as a coordinated campaign (timing) with independent version numbers.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-31): Tier 2 cross-package. Where Bit's algebraic structure (Boolean algebra -- lattice/semiring/monoid; the Z/2Z field) should live, and which of swift-bit-primitives' hand-rolled operations are redundant under the institute's established structure-as-witness-in-a-bridge pattern. FINDING (verified 2026-05-31 against source): swift-bit-primitives hand-rolls algebraic structure that the institute already models as composable inline witnesses in extracted {element}-algebra bridges -- Swift.Bool has its full tower witnessed in swift-bool-algebra-primitives (Algebra.{Lattice,Monoid,Semilattice,Semiring}+Bool, built from &&/||/!), whereas Bit has ONLY its Z/2Z field witnessed (swift-bit-algebra-primitives Bit+Z2.swift, inline from ^/&) and hand-rolls everything else in Bit Boolean Primitives + a Bit Z2 Field op-layer whose adding/multiplying are ORPHANED (no caller; Bit.z2 re-derives ^/& itself). RECONCILES with order-cluster-decomposition-and-modernization.md (Tier 3, plan-only): its C1-C5 placement law (Finite.Enumerable is a CAPABILITY not a home -- C2) and its generic swift-finite-algebra-primitives bridge (generic over Finite.Enumerable = Z/NZ cyclic via ordinal) mean Bit (count 2, Finite.Enumerable) gets Z/2Z for free once that bridge lands -- subsuming the per-type Bit.z2 to optional sugar. ANSWER to the framing question 'refactor Bit Boolean to use bool-algebra?': NOT depend on bool-algebra (Bool-specific; Bit is a distinct bit-domain type -- Option D rejected), but MIRROR bool-algebra's witness pattern for Bit in bit-algebra. RECOMMENDED Option C (staged toward B): (1) retire the orphaned Bit Z2 Field adding/multiplying; (2) author Bit's Algebra.{Lattice,Semilattice,Monoid,Semiring} witnesses inline in swift-bit-algebra-primitives, mirroring bool-algebra; (3) keep generators (^&|~) + ergonomic gates native in Bit Boolean (Bit is a hardware-logic type -- gates are domain vocabulary); (4) coordinate the Z/2Z FIELD subsumption + the Bit:Finite.Enumerable conformance placement with order-cluster Phase 1. bit->finite (the memory-readiness blocker) is governed by order-cluster's capability-placement: the conformance is incidental (C2), currently the SOLE reason bit depends on finite; it moves (to finite reaching down, or a bit-finite bridge) and finite leaves bit's -- and memory's -- transitive closure. [MOD-034]: Bit's Hash/Equation/Comparison conformances are FOUNDATIONAL and STAY in bit; only the incidental Boolean-algebra + Z/2Z structure goes to the bridge. Plan-only: no source changed; multi-package program surfaced for the principal to sequence per feedback_class_c_ecosystem_stop_not_dispatch.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-28): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide / cross-layer. The theoretically-optimal capability-protocol model for the data-structure stack (Memory.Contiguous / Storage / Buffer / Set + the iteration substrate) so a conformer inherits the maximum derivable surface for free, bounded by the specialization boundary. CORE MODEL: one HAS-A composition stack of MINIMAL capability cores (Memory.Contiguous.Protocol=span; Storage.Protocol=pointer(at:)+capacity; Buffer.Protocol=count; Set.Protocol=contains+count), over which EVERY derived-operation family is an ORTHOGONAL concern composed via `where Self: Core [& OtherConcern]`, NEVER baked into a core -- generalizing the ecosystem's 'iteration is an orthogonal sibling, not a refinement chain' stance from iteration to ALL derived families (iteration AND algebra). Three edge kinds: Refine (only within an axis where identity holds, e.g. Collection.Protocol: Iterable), Compose-default (across axes, where Self: Q), Provides-as-default (intra-core, e.g. isEmpty from count). The require/provide line is the SPECIALIZATION BOUNDARY (HARD constraint): derived/cold ops are protocol-extension defaults (proven to monomorphize to 0 witness_method), hot mutating ops stay concrete-Base Property.View (protocol-Base accessors need @inlinable -> the documented ~Copyable borrow-init miscompile). SET.PROTOCOL ELEVATION (the focus): reduced to its {contains, count} membership+cardinality CORE -- 'what makes a set'; enumeration is the orthogonal ITERATION concern (Iterable); ALGEBRA is the THIRD orthogonal concern, decomposed into a dedicated swift-set-algebra-primitives BRIDGE package (principal 2026-05-28) bridging swift-set-primitives with the swift-algebra-*-primitives family (union=join / intersection=meet over a bounded lattice; union/intersection/complement=Boolean algebra; subset=lattice order; laws via swift-algebra-law-primitives). This collapses today's three-location algebra fragmentation (predicates in set-primitives + Set.Ordered-returning constructive defaults in set-ordered + duplicate .algebra accessor) and fixes the hard-coded Set.Ordered return -> constructive ops return Self on a BuildableSet growable refinement. PILOT (Set.Ordered, /tmp/set-capability-pilot, Apple Swift 6.3.2): inherits-vs-writes table (OrderedSet writes 5 members + 3 conformance lines, inherits 9 algebra ops) + SIL receipt PASS -- inherited algebra + membership specialize to 0 witness_method on the hot path (release, cross-module), EVEN across the two-protocol (Set.Protocol & Iterating) composition (2 residual witnesses both in the unused generic fallback; whole-program=0); ~Copyable-element coverage proven (MoveOnlySet, no @_implements). RECONCILIATION (HANDOFF-013): EXTENDS the landed Buffer.Protocol (NOT superseded -- buffer arc not paused) + storage-generic-buffer-core two-lever model; COORDINATES with the LOCKED iteration arc (unified-iteration-design v1.3.0) -- narrow ~Copyable-predicate enumeration dependency on the sec 2.1 (borrowing Element) floor; SUPERSEDES only Set.Protocol's algebra bake-in+fragmentation; sequencer-refactor LANDED (worktree gone; Sequence.Protocol -> top-level Sequenceable on sequence-primitives main 309c1b9; v2 handoff gate lifted). APPROVED 2026-05-28 (supervisor): SIL receipt verified against the real file (hot-path 0-witness), sequencer-refactor landing + Buffer.Protocol-extended-not-superseded confirmed; §3 normal form + Set.Protocol elevation approved as the x16 fan-out template foundation; decisions #1/#3/#4 approved, #2 substrate RESOLVED (principal authorizes a bounded-lattice package swift-algebra-lattice = semilattice + absorption, + a Boolean-algebra package on Swift.Bool + the algebra family per [PKG-NAME-*], so the bridge witnesses union=join/intersection=meet/complement over real packaged structures). Research phase concluded. No production code landed, no live package/worktree edited; execution sequenced by the principal -- NOT begun.

documents.displayName: Order-Theoretic Cluster -- Decomposition, Composition, and Modernization
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-28): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Widens finite-interval-polarity-decomposition.md (which it SUPERSEDES) into a full reconciliation + [MOD-031] modernization of the order-theoretic cluster: swift-comparison-primitives (modern; the relation -- Comparison result + Comparison.Protocol/Comparable, intrinsic order), swift-order-primitives (NOT modern; arrangement -- Order.Comparator/Direction/Orderable/Projection, extrinsic order, depends on comparison), a NEW swift-interval-primitives (Interval.Bound/Boundary/Endpoint), swift-finite-primitives (NOT modern; the enumeration capability), and the classification overflow in swift-algebra-primitives (modern). First-principles Comparison<->Order assessment: the split is principled on an intrinsic-vs-extrinsic ordering axis (Comparison = the relation atom; Order = arrangement strategies built on it; Order->Comparison dependency correct), with two flagged defects: Order.Orderable is a near-vacuous empty marker (its extrinsic-orderability semantic undocumented), and the cluster is half-modernized (order+finite legacy) which is why placements drifted. Placement decisions: Bound/Boundary/Endpoint -> Interval.* in new swift-interval-primitives; Gradient is a DUPLICATE of the already-existing Order.Direction (asc/desc + .reversed) -> UNIFY, not a new Algebra Gradient target (revises the superseded doc's Q2); Monotonicity (algebra->Order.Monotonicity, 06-XX); Sign/Polarity/Ternary triage out of algebra (Phase 4); Parity stays (genuine Z/2Z element, only enum with packaged Algebra.Group/Field witnesses); Algebra.Iso stays but flagged vs Optic.Iso. PLACEMENT FRAMEWORK (sec 4): constitutive identity vs incidental property -- a type is homed by the concept it exists to express (C1), never by a capability it conforms to (C2: Finite.Enumerable is a capability, conformers span all domains -> 'Int belongs in Hashable' error), with adjacent-consensus (C3) outranking and primitiveness (C4) only a tiebreaker; this resolves the enums to their constitutive domains (interval/order), NOT finite. Z/Nz witnesses: ONE generic bridge swift-finite-algebra-primitives (deps finite+algebra+algebra-group), generic over Finite.Enumerable (an N-element finite enum = Z/Nz cyclic via ordinal; the existing per-type z2 witnesses already ARE this) -- subsumes the earlier per-domain bridges (no interval-algebra/order-algebra/algebra-finite); also homes the Algebra.Z<n>/Residue system; per-type .z2 sugar optional. The bridge name is correct per [PKG-NAME-016] because the RECIPIENT is the Finite.Enumerable CAPABILITY (any conformer), not the Finite domain -- so Order.Direction/Interval.Bound live in their constitutive domains yet receive algebra generically (C5). Target graph restores the invariant finite NO LONGER depends on algebra (finite reaches down to conform interval/comparison types to Finite.Enumerable; BOTH directions of finite-algebra integration -- generic witnesses AND algebra-classification : Finite.Enumerable conformances -- live in the one bridge). Phased execution plan, finite-FIRST (Phase 1 = remove finite's algebra dependency: create interval-primitives + the one finite-algebra bridge, DROP Gradient/keep Order.Direction, rewire finite Package.swift to drop algebra/algebra-group + add interval, delete finite's Algebra.Group+* witnesses); Phase 2 finite [MOD-031]; Phase 3 order [MOD-031] + Monotonicity inbound + optional Gradient-alias migration; Phase 4 algebra triage (Sign/Polarity/Ternary) + Order.Orderable + Algebra.Iso/Optic.Iso; Phase 5 Interval value type + unbounded axis. RESOLVED 2026-05-28: single generic bridge; keep Order.Direction drop Gradient; conformances preserved in bridge. EXECUTION DELEGATED (orchestrator/supervisor + subordinate/implementer via subagents or /handoff); plan-only, no source changed.

documents.displayName: Finite, Interval, and Polarity -- Decomposing the Four Z/2Z Enums
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-28): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. Where do Bound/Boundary/Endpoint/Gradient (and their Z/2Z witnesses) belong, given they are mis-parented in swift-finite-primitives (a Finite-namespaced package) when they conform to Finite.Enumerable only incidentally (like Polarity/Comparison)? Diagnosis: the four are one Z/2Z species but order-theoretic (MSC 06-XX) value-classifications, NOT finite-enumeration vocabulary; the Z/2Z algebra is a SECONDARY witness (bit-arc precedent: domain identity primary, algebra in a recipient-owned bridge). Cross-ecosystem SLR (Swift/Rust/C++ Boost.ICL/Haskell data-interval/PostgreSQL/IEEE 1788/MSC 2020, primary sources verified) shows: inclusivity (Boundary=open/closed) is first-class INTERVAL vocabulary (Haskell `data Boundary = Open|Closed` exact match), Bound x Boundary compose into an endpoint (Boost.ICL 4-valued interval_bounds), the word 'Bound' is 3-way overloaded (Swift RangeExpression.Bound=value, Rust Bound=inclusivity, institute Bound=which-end -> namespacing mandatory), and there is NO 'binary-classification' domain anywhere (the Z/2Z trait cuts across domains, per RES-029). RECOMMENDATION (Option B): (1) new swift-interval-primitives (namespace Interval) hosts Interval.Bound + Interval.Boundary + Interval.Endpoint (compositional endpoint descriptors; resolves the Bound collision; noun 'Interval' not 'Range'[taken]/'Order'[reserved super-domain]); (2) Gradient -> swift-algebra-primitives classification family beside Monotonicity (its 3-valued sibling; order-direction, not interval); (3) Z/2Z witnesses -> recipient-owned bridge swift-interval-algebra-primitives (Interval.Bound.z2 etc., like Bit.z2) for the interval enums + Algebra.Group+Gradient in swift-algebra-group-primitives (beside Algebra.Group+Parity) for Gradient; (4) finite-primitives reduces to the genuine (A) Finite-enumeration domain (Finite.Enumerable/Enumeration/Capacity + Finite.Bound<N> tag + Ordinal.Finite<N>/Index.Bounded<N>), MOD-035 scope statement + MOD-031 singular Finite Primitive root (note Core is NOT zero-dep, so no flat Core->Primitive rename); the +Finite conformances STAY (finite reaches down, as it already does for Polarity/Comparison), the Z/2Z witnesses LEAVE. Residual (surface-not-dispatch per feedback_class_c): whether Monotonicity/Gradient/Comparison should consolidate into an Order domain; the unbounded endpoint axis absent from binary Boundary. No skill amendment required. Change-no-source research doc.

This doc remains the detailed source for the cross-ecosystem SLR (Rust/Haskell/Boost.ICL/PostgreSQL/IEEE-1788/MSC, primary sources [Verified: 2026-05-28]), the bit-precedent framing, and the Bound-collision analysis. documents.topic: Should the feedback_clean_build_first.md memory rule be elevated from advisory ('rm -rf .build before debugging unexpected failures') to preemptive ('clean .build before verification when ecosystem churn has occurred since last build')? Two recent instances surfaced the recurring pattern: (1) Stream 2 polish dispatch swift-linter ld symbol-not-found from Binary primitives drift; (2) lint-manifest-drop dispatch swift-tagged-primitives duplicate-symbol from Binary_Borrowed_Primitives. Both verifications ran against .build directories whose age spanned multiple ecosystem churn events. The reflection's third-instance threshold suggests deferring elevation until a third independent instance surfaces. Open questions: detection mechanism, cost asymmetry, scope of preemptive rule, third-instance threshold.

documents.topic: Canonical catalog of pure-structural-extraction defect-detection patterns. The 2026-05-07 swift-linter modularization cohort surfaced three reusable patterns: (1) helper-duplication-after-extraction (Phase 2.5b: sanitize/tempPath duplicated across Lint.Driver and Manifest.Resolver), (2) concrete-string-leak-in-generic ('Lint.swift' string in Manifest.Resolver.evalParent leaking lint-specific knowledge into the supposedly generic resolver), (3) AST tokenKind discriminator under generic syntax types (rethrows accidentally flagged by Lint.Rule.UntypedThrows because both throws and rethrows are ThrowsClauseSyntax). Each has a grep-recipe detection. Open questions: pattern enumeration (other classes), detection automation, where the catalog lives (skill rule vs research doc vs Scripts/), ordering. Likely outcome: combination — research doc as audit trail, [MOD-026] rule for normative discipline, Scripts/ for automation.

documents.topic: Should the institute preserve, formally deprecate, or completely remove the single-file Lint.swift consumer form? Phase B.1 of the architecture cohort left the single-file path inert at post-decouple state (zero findings without consumer-side rule registration). The Lint/ nested-package shape is the working consumer pattern. D1' README cleanup stripped the inert documentation; this doc asks whether engine-side support should also be removed, deprecated with migration path, or preserved as a future-facing sugar form (typed-DSL evolution that subsumes Lint/ for simple cases). The Tier 1/Tier 2 canonical scaffolds currently use single-file form — removal would require their migration to Lint/. Pending investigation.

documents.topic: Pre-publishable evaluation of the cleanest possible consumer-facing syntax for the swift-linter ecosystem (engine `swift-foundations/swift-linter`, rule packs `swift-foundations/swift-linter-rules`, primitives `swift-primitives/swift-linter-primitives`) across the two consumer shapes that emerged via the architecture-cohort cleanup: (a) single-file `Lint.swift` declaring `let manifest: Lint.Manifest = …` evaluated by swift-manifest's subprocess loader; (b) nested SwiftPM `Lint/Package.swift` + `Lint/Sources/Lint/main.swift` whose executable IS the linter binary linking engine + rule packs. Premise correction per [RES-023]: the dispatching handoff's Current State described only the older init-kwargs Manifest shape; empirical state at HEAD 2026-05-07 shows TWO consumer-facing types coexisting by design — `Lint.Manifest` (typed `[Lint.Rule.ID]` via Tagged, JSON-serializable wire format) at L3 and `Lint.Configuration` (typed metatype DSL using `@Array<Lint.Rule.Configuration>.Builder` with `.enable(R.Type)` / `.disable(R.Type)` / `.override(R.Type, severity:)` factories, recursive parent inheritance via Ownership.Shared) at L1. Q1–Q6 sub-questions answered per dispatch brief: Q1a single-file Manifest = init-kwargs (current) + add `validate()` method; Q1b nested-package Configuration = current Array.Builder typed-metatype DSL, drop redundant `let manifest = Lint.Manifest(...)` declaration in PoC main.swift (eliminates current names-rule-twice defect with `if enabled.contains(R.id) { .enable(R.self) }` gating); Q1c cross-shape composition = Manifest is wire format + Configuration is in-process value + lift function for parent-chain hydration; Q2 rule authoring = struct + Lint.Rule.Protocol (status quo correct — macros add maintenance burden across rule packs without algebraic gain); Q3 per-rule parameters = pre-construct rule INSTANCE via new `.enable<R>(_ instance: R)` factory + optional `instance` field on Rule.Configuration (defer concrete migration until first parameterized rule lands per [IMPL-001] principled absence); Q4 severity/exclusions = status quo + v2/v3 path-typing alignment when Copyable typed-path L1 ships; Q5 parent-chain = wire format Manifest + lift via local rule registry, single mechanism across both shapes; Q6 citations = introduce `Lint.Citation` enum at L1 + split `static let message` into `static let citation: Lint.Citation` + `static let messageBody: Swift.String` (v2 before broad adoption, mechanical migration ~15 rules). v1/v2/v3 phasing locks forward shape; v1 changes are minimal (drop redundant Manifest in PoC, add Manifest.validate); v2 lands lift function + Citation split + instance-taking factory; v3 triggers when first parameterized rule or Copyable typed-path lands. Prior Art Survey [RES-021] across SwiftLint custom_rules YAML, swift-format `.swift-format` JSON, ESLint plugin packages, Rust Clippy + dylint, SwiftSyntax test harness, institute result-builder ecosystem — each surveyed with concrete syntax examples and what to learn / avoid. Theoretical grounding [RES-022]: wire-format vs object-graph distinction (Codable, Protobuf descriptor / typed-message, JSON Schema / typed-value precedents). Cognitive Dimensions empirical validation [RES-025]: all six dimensions favour the recommended shape.

Prior research consulted per [RES-019]: swiftsyntax-based-custom-linter-investigation.md v1.0.0 (RECOMMENDATION 2026-05-06) locks Option C standalone CLI + Option F symbol-graph extension; developer-tool-package-architecture.md DECISION Tier 3 establishes Developer/standalone shape. Implementation hooks documented inline for v1 dispatch (drop redundant Manifest decl ~50 lines in PoC main.swift; add Manifest.validate ~30 lines). v2/v3 changes documented as forward-shape locks. 3 open questions surfaced for the implementing dispatch (lift signature, Citation case set, v3 trigger ordering).

documents.topic: Canonical pattern for tools deployed beyond the developer's workspace, when the tool's runtime needs a path / config / endpoint that varies per adopter. Origin: swift-linter Driver hardcoded fallback to /Users/coen/Developer/swift-foundations/swift-linter when SWIFT_LINTER_PATH unset; the fallback worked in dev but would silently misbehave on first non-developer adopter run. Question axes: env-var-or-fail vs env-var-or-fallback; acceptable fallback shapes (XDG, CWD-walk, well-known install location); diagnostic discipline (silent vs warn vs hard-fail); detection at release-readiness time. Four options: A (env-var-or-fail), B (env-var-or-warn-and-empty-default — current post-fix shape), C (XDG fallback), D (CWD-walk per Cargo/npm convention). Pending investigation.
documents.statusDetail: v0.1.0. Tier 2 ecosystem-wide research. Authored in response to action item from Research/Reflections/2026-05-07-swift-linter-pre-publishable-discovery-investigation.md. Open questions span detection, fallback shapes, diagnostic discipline, and release-readiness gate.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 cross-package unified design Research for two L1 primitives packages, applying the framework codified in 2026-05-12-typed-identifier-naming-framework.md. Package 1 — swift-package-primitives: top-level Package.* / Product.* / Target.* namespaces; v1.0.0 trio Package.Name + Product.Name + Target.Name as pure typealiases over Tagged<_, Swift.String>; no validation at construction (mirrors upstream permissive tier); inherits conformances from Tagged main+SLI; greenfield with SE-0292 DocC citation; primitives Tier 1 (dep on swift-tagged-primitives). Package 2 — swift-version-primitives: Version.* namespace; v1.0.0 Version.Semantic struct (NOT typealias — has spec invariants), Version.Semantic.Identifier enum, Version.Semantic.Error enum; throwing init with typed throws; hand-rolled conformances per SemVer 2.0.0 §10 build-metadata-excluded equality; greenfield with semver.org+SE-0292+SE-0152 DocC citations; primitives Tier 0 or 1. SUPERSEDES four prior 2026-05-12 docs: 2026-05-12-swift-spm-primitives-design.md (parent, commit 14df127), validation-timing, target-name-v1, swift-semver-primitives-package-version-placement (all at commit adb87d8). Substantive recommendations of all four prior docs survive; only naming changes (SwiftPM.* → Package.*, swift-spm-primitives → swift-package-primitives, swift-semver-primitives → swift-version-primitives, SemVer.Version → Version.Semantic). Closes audit defers F-A2.11 / F-A3.4 / F-A3.5. Three consumers verified for Package.Name+Product.Name (manifest-primitives, manifests, linter) + one independent consumer for Target.Name (swift-dependency-analysis — Package.Manifest.Target.name + Package.analyze.swift:60/80/82). swift-version-primitives' v1.0.0 second-consumer hurdle deferred to its own future dispatch per framework Axiom 4. L3 swift-dependency-analysis Package namespace requires migration in downstream dispatch. swift-foundations/swift-linter currently being worked on per user — audit-closure migration should coordinate.

Per [RES-026] citations: swiftlang/swift-package-manager@main e1ced73eb 2026-05-12 (PackageIdentity.swift:130-294, Manifest.swift:31, PackageBuilder.swift:837-844/1576-1578, Version.swift:38-93), swift-tools-support-core Version.swift:49-167, SE-0152/0292/0450, semver.org. Per [RES-022] structural-correctness framing: typealias shape (Package.Name trio) and validating-struct shape (Version.Semantic) chosen on merits not diff-size — Package.Name trio matches permissive upstream tier + Lint.Rule.ID precedent; Version.Semantic struct shape matches spec-strict Glob.Pattern precedent because semver.org IS strict. Four open questions surfaced for principal sign-off (flat vs nested namespace, Version.Semantic struct vs typealias, v1.0.0 Range/Set inclusion, Tier 2 vs 3). Four downstream dispatches recommended (package authoring × 2; L3 Package-namespace migration; linter audit-closure migration coordinated with in-flight work).

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 cross-package companion to 2026-05-12-swift-spm-primitives-design.md addressing open question #3 (SwiftPM.Package.Version placement). Substantiates the parent doc's deferral via cross-ecosystem prior-art survey and naming-convention analysis. Recommends Option C: separate swift-semver-primitives package owns SemVer.Version + adjacent types (Version.Range, Version.Set, Error); swift-spm-primitives v1.0.0 ships WITHOUT a Version type; v1.1.0+ MAY add SwiftPM-flavored dependency-requirement composition over SemVer.Version. Cross-ecosystem precedent unanimous (per subagent survey 2026-05-12): Rust semver crate, Node node-semver, Dart pub_semver all ship separate-from-PM. SwiftPM is the outlier — duplicate Version structs in PackageDescription DSL (Sources/Runtimes/PackageDescription/Version.swift:38-93) + TSCUtility (TSC's typed-throws variant) is direct empirical evidence that bundling failed in Swift. Community Swift SemVer libraries (mxcl/Version 336 stars; SwiftPackageIndex/SemanticVersion 70 stars; sersoft-gmbh/semver imports Foundation incompat with [PRIM-FOUND-001]; gwynne/swift-semver) exist precisely because SwiftPM's is not consumable outside the manifest DSL. No SE proposal codifies SemVer rules; SE-0152 references semver.org with patch-optional amendment. Per [API-NAME-003] spec-mirroring: namespace SemVer (matches semver.org short form), package swift-semver-primitives, type SemVer.Version. Three options enumerated: A inline in swift-spm-primitives (rejected — repeats SwiftPM's duplication mistake), B separate package (recommended core), C separate + explicit boundary that SwiftPM-flavored composition lives in swift-spm-primitives (recommended refined). This Research does NOT author swift-semver-primitives; recommends future Research+package-authoring dispatch when [RES-018] second-consumer verified at that dispatch's write time (candidates: Swift Package Index, release tooling, registry-aware tooling, NPM/Cargo bridges).

Per [RES-026] citations: SemVer 2.0.0 spec semver.org, Rust semver docs.rs, Node node-semver GitHub, swiftlang/swift-package-manager Version sources at file:line, swiftlang/swift-tools-support-core duplicate Version, SE-0152 tools-version. Per [RES-022] structural-correctness framing: cross-ecosystem precedent + spec-mirroring discipline + SwiftPM's own internal duplication evidence dominate ecosystem-package-footprint concerns. Recommended SemVer.Version shape sketched for future dispatch (NOT authored here): 5-component struct, throwing init with typed throws, PreReleaseIdentifier enum, Range/Set algebra. Three open questions for principal sign-off (Option C over B — explicit composition-boundary principle; SemVer namespace over SemanticVersion/SemVer_2_0_0; future-dispatch shape full-Research vs abbreviated-brief).

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 cross-package design Research authoring the new L1 primitives package `swift-spm-primitives` to type SwiftPM identifier concepts. Closes the primitives-gap blocker on three Ambiguous findings (F-A2.11, F-A3.4, F-A3.5) deferred by the 2026-05-12 typed-primitive adoption audit + addendum. Recommends: package name `swift-spm-primitives`; namespace `SwiftPM.*` (Apple-canonical prose form, spec-mirroring per [API-NAME-003], CamelCase acronym precedent SwiftUI/SwiftSyntax); v1.0.0 type trio `SwiftPM.Package.Name`, `SwiftPM.Product.Name`, `SwiftPM.Target.Name` — pure typealiases over `Tagged<_, Swift.String>` (matches Lint.Rule.ID precedent); no validation at construction (mirrors upstream SwiftPM's emptiness-only manifest-level rule, compatible with institute product-name convention of spaces e.g. `Manifest Primitives`); conformances inherited from Tagged main + SLI (Sendable, Hashable, Equatable, Comparable, Codable, CustomStringConvertible, ExpressibleByStringLiteral); greenfield with SE-0292 citation in DocC (no upstream code borrowed at v1.0.0 since typealias-only); Tier 2 per [RES-020]; primitives Tier 1 (single dep on swift-tagged-primitives). Three consumers identified clearing [RES-018] second-consumer hurdle: L1 swift-manifest-primitives (Manifest.Dependency.{name,product}), L3 swift-manifests (transitive), L3 swift-foundations/swift-linter (originating). Upstream validation landscape verified against swiftlang/swift-package-manager@main e1ced73eb 2026-05-12: two-tier strict (SE-0292 Scope/Name) vs permissive (manifest-level Package/Product/Target name with emptiness only); v1.0.0 inherits permissive tier. Roadmap: SwiftPM.Registry.Scope/Name (SE-0292), Manifest.ToolsVersion, Source.Kind enum deferred to v1.1.0+ on consumer demand; SwiftPM.Package.Version explicitly defers to future swift-semver-primitives (cross-ecosystem domain). Recommended next dispatch: package-authoring brief implementing the recommended shape verbatim.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 cross-package companion to 2026-05-12-swift-spm-primitives-design.md addressing open question #2 (Target.Name v1.0.0 inclusion). Verifies that SwiftPM.Target.Name clears [RES-018]'s second-consumer hurdle INDEPENDENTLY of Package.Name and Product.Name, justifying inclusion in the v1.0.0 trio on substantive grounds rather than symmetric-completeness grounds. Critical finding: the originating audit consumer (swift-foundations/swift-linter) does NOT consume target names from manifests — Lint.SingleFile.Materializer hardcodes target name as 'Lint', and Lint.SingleFile.Extractor extracts package+product names but NOT target names from consumer Lint.swift AST [Verified: 2026-05-12]. INDEPENDENT consumer is swift-foundations/swift-dependency-analysis: Manifest.Target.name used at Package.analyze.swift:60 (analysis identity), :80 (Tests/<target> path construction), :82 (Sources/<target> path construction) [Verified: 2026-05-12]. Hurdle cleared decisively. Refines parent doc's imprecise consumer citation (parent claimed manifest-primitives + swift-manifests reference target names; verified manifest-primitives.Dependency has package name + product name + path + imports but NO target-name field, and swift-manifests does not extract target names from consumer manifests). Four options enumerated: A trio (recommended), B audit-minimal pair, C extended (trio + Source.Kind + ToolsVersion — fails [RES-018]), D trio + advisory roadmap section. Recommends Option D: trio + explicit README roadmap deferring Source.Kind/ToolsVersion/Registry.*/Identity/Version to v1.1.0+ on demonstrated consumer demand. Suggests parent doc may want v1.1.0 amendment citing swift-dependency-analysis evidence verbatim.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 cross-package companion to 2026-05-12-swift-spm-primitives-design.md addressing open question #1 (validation timing). Recommends Option A: pure typealias `SwiftPM.{Package,Product,Target}.Name = Tagged<_, Swift.String>` with NO validation at construction. Rationale per [RES-022] structural correctness: (1) Lint.Rule.ID precedent (canonical institute typealias-without-validation shape); (2) upstream SwiftPM's manifest-level validators enforce only emptiness — adding stricter institute rules over-applies validation where the spec is permissive; (3) institute product-name convention (spaces e.g. "Manifest Primitives") would be forbidden by any meaningfully stricter validator; (4) type discrimination (the audit's actual goal — F-A2.11/F-A3.4/F-A3.5 are API mix-up risks not value malformation risks) is delivered regardless of validation. Four options enumerated: A typealias (recommended), B validating struct (Glob.Pattern shape), C hybrid typealias + .validate() method, D typealias at v1.0.0 with future companion Strict struct in v1.1.0+. Cognitive-dimensions analysis per [RES-025]: Option A under-abstracts on error-proneness but matches institute consistency; Option B matches error-proneness but over-applies relative to upstream's discipline. DocC note SHOULD make permissive contract explicit so future readers don't assume the typed wrapper validates. Future-proofs an additive Strict wrapper if a v1.1.0+ consumer surfaces real construction-time enforcement need.

Per [RES-025] cognitive dimensions framework: per-option assessment on visibility / consistency / viscosity / role-expressiveness / error-proneness / abstraction. documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide meta-research codifying the typed-identifier naming framework with four axioms: (1) names spelled out fully — no abbreviations as primary identifiers (typealiases for additive convenience post-v1.0.0 permitted); (2) top-level namespace is the most-generic English noun for the domain entity, not the brand/tool/consumer (maximal-reuse principle made structural); (3) specialization narrows via nested types within the generic namespace (variants accrete additively; spec-mirroring [API-NAME-003] applies inside the namespace, not at it); (4) inclusion at any version is gated on second-consumer demand verified empirically at write time ([RES-018] applied to typed-identifier primitives). Triggered by principal feedback on 2026-05-12-swift-spm-primitives-design.md (commit 14df127): no-abbreviations rule + maximal ecosystem adoption/reuse/integration. Framework is partly NEW codification (generic-noun-first framing) + partly making implicit institute patterns explicit (swift-foundations/swift-dependency-analysis already uses Package.* at L3; swift-manifest-primitives uses Manifest.* at L1). Carve-outs documented: typealias convenience post-v1.0.0; spec-mirroring acronyms RFC_4122/ISO_32000/IEEE_754; institute-grandfathered acronyms IO and similar. Cross-references existing rules: [API-NAME-001]/[001a]/[002]/[003]/[010]/[012]/[013], [PKG-NAME-001], [PRIM-NAME-001], [RES-018]. Worked examples: Version.Semantic not SemVer; Package.Name not SwiftPM.Package.Name. Future work: skill-promotion candidate for code-surface as [API-NAME-NNN]; lint-rule candidate Lint.Rule.Naming.AbbreviationAsPrimary; existing-precedent audit pass for abbreviation debt. Companion to 2026-05-12-swift-package-and-version-primitives-design.md which APPLIES the framework. Supersedes naming choices in four prior 2026-05-12 docs.

Four open questions for principal sign-off (axiom confirmation, carve-out boundaries, Tier 2 vs 3 classification, skill-promotion path).

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide audit extending the v1.2.0 noncopyable-adoption-targets-ecosystem-survey (linter / source-primitives scope) across ecosystem corners — file-system, kernel, IO, threads, process, sockets, source, parsers, lexer, parser-machine, binary-parser, storage, buffer, async, text, string, input, time. Surveys all currently-Copyable resource-bearing or large-value-typed candidates against the v1.2.0 six-axis scoring framework PLUS a new Wave-5+6 ecosystem-readiness lens classifying each candidate's cascade shape (closure-inference / inout-discipline / single-instance / stdlib-array-storage / stdlib-protocol / Codable-wire-format / cross-thread-shared). Adds Rows 11–18 to the predecessor's Rows 1–10. Principal finding: ZERO new top-tier candidates — the ecosystem has already adopted ~Copyable on essentially every type where structural shape allows. Wave 5+6's ~3× under-estimate came from TWO SPECIFIC absorbable-cascade shapes (closure-inference for the 73-rule Lint.Rule.findings cascade; inout-discipline for Source.Manager's call sites); the dominant residual Copyable surface is structurally gated. Highest-scoring structural candidates (recorded but NOT recommended without gating decision): Row 11 Parser.Machine.Compiled 14/30 (same shape as v1.2.0 Row 1 — reference-typed Cache in Copyable boundary — but blocked on Parser.Protocol ~Copyable relaxation across the parser stack, a Tier-3 decision); Row 13 File.Directory.Contents.Iterator 12/30 (strongest resource-correlation 5/5 + existing-alignment 5/5; IteratorHandle workaround at line 18 is a direct admission of the ~Copyable intent; blocked on upstream Swift Evolution ~Copyable IteratorProtocol/Sequence). Not-a-candidate rows for completeness: Row 16 Kernel.Thread.Handle.Reference (already a Copyable bridge over ~Copyable Kernel.Thread.Handle per the L3-Copyable-wraps-L1-~Copyable pattern); Row 17 sync primitives Kernel.Thread.{Semaphore,Synchronization,Gate,Barrier} (cross-thread-shared by design per [MEM-SAFE-024] Category C); Row 18 heap storage Storage.{Slab,Pool,Heap,Arena,Split} (ManagedBuffer-backed COW; companion ~Copyable Storage.{Inline,Pool.Inline,Arena.Inline} already exists). Deferred Row 14 Text.Line.Map 2/30 (stdlib-Array storage barrier + zero resource correlation) and Row 15 Source.Cache 1/30 (stdlib-Dictionary storage barrier + explicit value-type design choice). Generalization-or-accident verdict on Wave 5+6 calibration: SPECIFIC, not general — fresh calibration data is NOT a license to expand the recommendation set, must be re-verified per-candidate against the cascade-shape table. Already-~Copyable control set extended beyond v1.2.0's: Source.Manager (Wave 6), Process.Handle, Sockets.TCP.Connection, Kernel.Thread.Worker, Kernel.Event.{Source,Driver}, Kernel.Completion+Driver+Notification, Terminal.Mode.Raw.Token, IO.Completion.Entry, String+Borrowed, Lexer.Scanner, Input.Slice+Buffer+Protocols, Buffer.Slots+Ring.Inline+Small, Storage.Inline+Pool.Inline+Arena.Inline, Async.Channel.{Bounded,Unbounded,Take,Ends}+Mutex.Local+Waiter.Entry/Queue.Flagged/Resumption+Lifecycle.State.Shutdown, Parser.Machine.Builder, Binary.Bytes.Input.View+Machine.Builder. Per [RES-019] internal grep: extends predecessor v1.2.0 + noncopyable-ecosystem-state v1.0.0 + se-0499-implications v1.3.0 + path-type-ecosystem-model + 6 supporting prior-research docs.

Per [RES-021] prior-art survey + contextualization step: Rust process::Child / TcpStream / JoinHandle / fs::ReadDir set as canonical resource-correlation reference; Rust Iterator trait's move-only-without-Copy precedent identified as the upstream-evolution model for Row 13's gating decision; Linear Haskell multiplicity polymorphism contextualizes Row 11's single-vs-shared bifurcation; C++ unique_ptr/shared_ptr single-impl-two-wrappers pattern shows the institute is already correctly applying the bifurcation where needed (Path/Paths.Path, Compiled/Prepared, Thread.Handle/Reference). Per [RES-025] Cognitive Dimensions: visibility (cascade-shape table foregrounds gating barriers), consistency (Row IDs continue v1.2.0 numbering), viscosity (NOT-A-CANDIDATE rows preempt re-asking), role-expressiveness (each row names its structural barrier), error-proneness (Wave-5+6 generalization-or-accident section closes off the foreseeable wrong inference). Per [RES-027] loose-end follow-up: all open questions are directions, not premises; principal finding is itself the deliverable. documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 2 cross-package survey of which types in swift-linter / swift-source-primitives / swift-foundations/swift-linter / swift-linter-rules / swift-institute-linter-rules / swift-parsers / swift-file-system should adopt ~Copyable under SE-0499's stdlib-protocol relaxation. Six-axis scoring (resource-correlation, size/hot-path, safety bug class, cascade cost, pattern-establishing, existing alignment) ranks Lint.Source.Parsed 25/30 as top pick (real per-file resource shape, large parsed-AST payload, pattern-establishing across all linter rules, mechanical 73-rule cascade, zero protocol losses since not Codable/Hashable today); Source.Manager 20/30 as second pick (single-registry semantic, large content payload, engine already passes inout, swiftc/Clang precedent); Lint.Run.Outcome 11/30 as profile-driven deferred. Confirms the brief's hypothesis: Source.Location-first (8/30) is the worst pick on cascade-to-payback ratio — 169 references, zero resource-correlation, Codable wire-format dependency in Test.Source.Location survives SE-0499. Already-~Copyable control set (File.Handle, File.Descriptor, Path_Primitives.Path, Path.Borrowed, File.Directory.Iterator) cited as canonical reference shape. Prior art per [RES-021]: Rust std::fs::File / rustc_span SourceFile / Linear Haskell / C++ unique_ptr; contextualization step argues for higher adopt/don't-adopt threshold than Rust-translation would suggest (Swift's per-type opt-in vs Rust's universal default). Adoption plan sketch for Lint.Source.Parsed: 6-8 hours single principal-authorization. Five open questions: 6.3-vs-6.4 gate (recommend 6.3+), visibility-method conversion, SwiftSyntax + ~Copyable storage interaction, Source.Manager bundling, @Sendable + borrowing closure-parameter composition. Per [RES-019] internal grep: extends noncopyable-ecosystem-state.md (DECISION) + se-0499-implications-for-equation-hash-comparison-primitives.md (RECOMMENDATION) + source-location-unification.md (DECISION, where the original 'Copyable cascade' constraint was the pre-SE-0499 framing this survey re-evaluates) + path-type-ecosystem-model.md (the L3-Copyable-wraps-L1-~Copyable reference architectural shape).
