documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.2.0): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide investigation resolving the 2026-05-13 ecosystem-corners audit's Q1 deferral on whether to relax Parser.`Protocol` to ~Copyable (Option α) and/or ~Escapable (Option β) on Self across the parser-stack ecosystem. v1.2.0 STRATIFIED-ARCHITECTURE REFRAMING (2026-05-14): Phase 2 protocol-only relaxation EXECUTED at swift-parser-primitives commit 3ed1961 (Parser.`Protocol`: ~Copyable + Parser.Printer: ~Copyable; 2 file changes vs v1.1.0 projection of 111; 163/163 tests; all 4 downstream packages build green). Empirical finding: v1.1.0 cascade projection conflated Phase 2 (protocol-only) with Phase 3 (combinator-level) — Phase 3 NOT required for Row 11 because Parser.Machine.Compiled is a TERMINAL runtime parser, not a participant in combinator chains. Combinator cascade attempted and reverted (correctly); combinators stay Copyable-only via implicit constraint; .error.map { } syntax preserved. Option A (`@_owned consuming get` on protocol extension) EMPIRICALLY REFUTED via /experiment-process at swift-parser-primitives/Experiments/owned-consuming-get-on-protocol-extension/ (commit 2221537): Swift 6.3.1/6.3.2 reject @_owned as unknown attribute; 6.4-dev passes only inside consuming-parameter wrapper helpers, fails at direct call-site with 'borrowed by non-Escapable type'. Compiler crash discovered on `consume c` + @_owned (SIL verifier abort at MemoryLifetimeVerifier.cpp:263) — deferred to issue-investigation. [RES-018] second-consumer framing rejected per principal feedback_correctness_and_evergreen.md. Recommendation supersedes v1.1.0 Option δ: adopt α-stratified — protocol relaxation at protocol level only; combinators stay Copyable; ~Copyable conformers exist as terminals. Phase 4 (Parser.Machine.Compiled: ~Copyable, Row 11) becomes immediate next executable phase, blocked only on parallel-session coordination in swift-parser-machine-primitives. v1.1.0 EMPIRICAL UPDATE (preserved per [RES-008]): v1.1.0 EMPIRICAL UPDATE: the v1.0.0 dominant cost item — cross-module witness-table SIGSEGV per swiftlang/swift#85441 pattern documented at swift-parser-primitives/Research/witness-table-sigsegv-with-noncopyable-protocol-constraints.md (Swift 6.2.3) — has been empirically RESOLVED via /experiment-process. Verification experiment at swift-parser-primitives/Experiments/parser-protocol-self-noncopyable-witness-tables/ ran six variants (V1 leaf construction, V2 leaf parse dispatch, V3 composed Map dispatch, V4 triple-nested composition, V5 with full original-crash conditions including external-package Input.Slice<Buffer<UInt8>.Linear>, V6 Parser.Lazy closure-of-~Copyable-return) on three toolchains (6.2.3 / 6.3.2 / 6.4-dev nightly 2026-05-07). ALL PASS on Swift 6.3.2 — the original production crash is irreproducible under the current production target toolchain. Revised six-axis scoring: Option α (Self: ~Copyable) = 6/30 (was 5/30; cascade cost 4/5 was 5/5 after SIGSEGV downgrade); Option β (Self: ~Copyable & ~Escapable) = 0/30 floored (unchanged; structurally untenable); Option γ = 7/30 (was 6/30; same SIGSEGV downgrade applies); Option δ (defer until [RES-018] second-consumer hurdle clears) = no cascade cost, RECOMMENDED. Sibling-type workaround Parser.Machine.OwnedCompiled framing from v1.0.0 DROPPED per feedback_no_sibling_type_workarounds.md (2026-05-13) — protocol-level relaxation is the architectural goal, not opt-in escape valves. Row 11's Cache aliasing stays as documented invariant at Parser.Machine.Compiled.swift:27–31 until protocol relaxation viable on merits. Dominant residual blockers: (1) 166-site mechanical cascade ceremony (each combinator extension needs explicit `where Upstream: ~Copyable` per the feedback_extension_implies_copyable gotcha hit at experiment compile time); (2) Parser.OneOf.Any.parsers: [Closure] hard structural cap (stdlib Array<~Copyable> not supported); (3) [RES-018] second-consumer hurdle NOT cleared. Revised re-evaluation triggers: (1) second parser-stack consumer surfaces — NOW DOMINANT GATE; (2) stdlib Array adds ~Copyable element support; (3) ecosystem authors new resource-correlated parser package. Phased migration plan (if reconsidered) provided: 6 phases starting with ~25 leaf parsers, scaling through 20+15 combinators, builder generic functions, type-erased opt-out, and Row 11 adoption — estimate ~8–16 hours mechanical migration once justified. Conformer cascade shape characterization: ~25 leaf parsers, ~20 single-parser combinators, ~15 N-parser combinators, 1 type-erased combinator hard cap, 2 Machine wrappers. Parser.Span.swift:48 'extension Parser.\`Protocol\` where Input: Parser.Input.\`Protocol\` & Copyable' constraint already partitions Copyable / ~Copyable at the Input axis at extension scope. Per [RES-021] contextualization: Rust nom/chumsky/combine adopt move-only Self because Rust's protocol-Self default IS move-only, not parser-semantics-driven; Haskell parsec/megaparsec don't have ownership axis; swift-parsing keeps Copyable Self matching Swift defaults. Universal adoption ≠ universal necessity. Per [RES-023] SLR (Hutton-Meijer 1996, Leijen-Meijer 2001, Krishnaswami-Yallop PLDI 2019): NO parser-combinator library adopts move-only Self because parser semantics demand it. Per [RES-024] formal semantics: typing rule + cascade rule formalized; operational semantics for Row 11 consume-prevents-aliasing sketched. Per [RES-025] Cognitive Dimensions evaluation. Per [RES-019] internal grep: cites and extends 11 prior research docs across swift-institute + 3 parser-package Research dirs (witness-table-sigsegv, parser-input-noncopyable-support, machine-noncopyable-input); supersedes none. Per [HANDOFF-013] reader-side prior-research check + [EXP-001] empirical verification + [HANDOFF-019] commit-as-you-go.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide design Research resolving Thread H.3's class-(c) escalation (File.Path.Temporary.randomized adoption blocked on swift-rfc-4122 build failure). Recommends Option B: create new L3 unifier swift-uuids composing swift-rfc-4122 × swift-rfc-9562 × swift-random after repairing swift-rfc-4122 to drop forbidden L2-to-L2 lateral deps (darwin-standard / linux-standard / windows-32) per [ARCH-LAYER-001], remove stale Darwin_Primitives / Linux_Primitives / Windows_Primitives imports per project_platform_stack_l2_reclassification.md, and remove stale 'Linux Kernel Standard' product reference (decomposed to per-domain granular products). Mirrors canonical swift-random L3-unifier shape per [PLAT-ARCH-009]; keeps RFC_4122.UUID canonical at L2 per [API-NAME-003] (no name inversion). Four options enumerated: A minimal repair (REJECTED — abandons v4/v7 affordance, violates [ARCH-LAYER-011]); B swift-uuids L3 unifier (RECOMMENDED); C swift-uuids fully owns UUID (REJECTED — violates [API-NAME-003] + [PLAT-ARCH-018]); D per-version split (REJECTED — sprawl without [RES-018] second consumer). Comparison table across 10 criteria covering layering cleanliness, ergonomics, cascade depth, future-extensibility, and conformance with [PLAT-ARCH-009] / [API-NAME-003] / [ARCH-LAYER-001] / [ARCH-LAYER-011] / [RES-018]. Prior art per [RES-021] (RFC 4122/9562, Apple Foundation, Rust uuid crate, Boost.UUID, Go google/uuid, Node crypto.randomUUID, OpenSSL); contextualization step shows Rust/Boost/Go pattern fits Swift Institute layering, Apple Foundation/Node monolithic pattern is the ANTI-pattern. Wave 4 phase plan: Phase 4.1 swift-rfc-4122 layering repair (drop 3 deps + 3 imports + native-syscall fast-path; ~50 LoC delete; restores standalone build), Phase 4.2 swift-uuids package creation + swift-identities migration (~200 LoC new + ~25 LoC delete; collapses Identity.UUID.random's 30-LoC inline body to delegate), Phase 4.3 File.Path.Temporary.randomized adoption (~30 LoC; clears Thread H.3 gate). Two class-(c) sub-issues surfaced and addressed inline: (1) latent CSPRNG defect at RFC_4122.Random.liveValue (Phase 4.1 upgrades to fatalError forcing L3 binding), (2) swift-identities carries dead-code UUID generation inline (Phase 4.2 migration). Out of scope: v1/v2/v6 generators (need stable host-id ecosystem), batch v7, cross-platform MD5/SHA-1 liveValue. Per [RES-019] internal prior-research grep: ZERO matches for uuid/rfc-4122/swift-uuids — greenfield design.

Per [RES-022] structural-correctness framing: Option B wins on layering cleanliness (mirror of [PLAT-ARCH-009] swift-random shape) + spec-mirroring discipline ([API-NAME-003] RFC_4122.UUID stays canonical at L2), not on diff size. Per [RES-018] second-consumer hurdle: cleared with swift-identities + swift-file-system. Per [RES-019] internal grep verified greenfield. Per [RES-023] empirical-claim verification at write-time: all source citations (Package.swift line numbers, source file paths, dep set, product names) verified against current ecosystem state 2026-05-13.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-15): Tier 2 cross-package research closing direction D6 of 2026-05-15-swift-arguments-ecosystem-design.md. Recommends Option A single always-async Command.Protocol with mutating func run() async throws(Command.Error); no Command.Async.Protocol. Rationale: swift-argument-parser's sync/async split was Swift 5.5/5.6 toolchain-availability-driven (verified at git PR #404 commit 1141ed1, 2022-03-14), not structural; institute precedent (Parser.Protocol, Serializer.Protocol, Coder.Protocol) is unanimously single-shape; all 3 existing institute CLIs already use AsyncParsableCommand. Composes orthogonally with D8 ~Copyable Command.Resource.Protocol axis. Prior-art survey: optparse-applicative, clap, System.CommandLine Beta 4 single-surface collapse, Cobra, Click.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-22): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide impact assessment for relocating `var count: Cardinal { consuming get }` off `Sequence.\`Protocol\`` (swift-sequence-primitives Sequence.Protocol+Count.swift:21). Triggered by sibling investigation `swift-buffer-primitives/HANDOFF-buffer-borrowing-count-sequence-detachment.md` Findings (RECOMMENDATION 2026-05-22) which traced 9+ test compile errors (`'buffer' used after consume`) on Buffer.Ring.Inline / Ring.Small / Linked.Inline / Linear.Inline to overload-resolution shadowing: the protocol-default's `where Self: ~Copyable, Element: Copyable` clause is strictly more specific than the typed accessor's `where Element: ~Copyable` clause, so Swift picks the consuming `count: Cardinal` over the typed `count: Index<Element>.Count { borrowing get }`. Principal reframe: local conformer-drop fix vs first-principles relocation. Three options evaluated per `[RES-029]` semantic-identity-first ranking + `[RES-022]` structural-correctness-over-diff-size: (a) move count to Collection.\`Protocol\` matching Apple stdlib's placement; (b) new opt-in sub-protocol Sequence.Counting.\`Protocol\`; (c) remove default entirely. Workspace enumeration: 73 direct Sequence.\`Protocol\` conformers — 40 Group A multi-pass indexed storage (Buffer 8, Queue 8, Array 4, Stack 4, Set.Ordered 4, Dictionary.Ordered 5, Heap 5, Vector 2, Bit.Vector 6, Input.Slice 1), 32 Group B lazy single-pass combinators (Sequence.{Map,Filter,Drop,Prefix,CompactMap,FlatMap,Difference,...}), 1 Group C. Only 3 of 40 Group A currently also conform to Collection.\`Protocol\` (Queue.DoubleEnded, Queue.DoubleEnded.Fixed, Buffer.Linear via Buffer.Linear+Collection.Protocol.swift); remaining ~36 are Collection.Protocol-eligible per pre-existing class-(c)-queued migration cited in count-direct-vs-fluent-and-hint-namespace.md v1.0.0 (DECISION 2026-05-21 commit ac09a43). Zero workspace generic-context call sites consume seq.count on a `some Sequence.\`Protocol\``-constrained receiver (per `[RES-023]` verified via grep across swift-primitives/swift-standards/swift-foundations/swift-institute Sources/; matches prior DECISION's 2026-05-21 grep). Apple stdlib precedent: Sequence has underestimatedCount: Int (hint, default 0) and NO count; Collection has count: Int (O(n) forward, O(1) RandomAccess) — institute deviated from this placement on stdlib-mental-model-match-for-call-site-shape grounds while putting count on the wrong protocol per stdlib's structural placement. Cross-protocol consistency check: Sequence.Borrowing.\`Protocol\` / Sequence.Drain.\`Protocol\` / Sequence.Clearable / Sequence.Consume.\`Protocol\` / Sequence.Iterator.\`Protocol\` carry zero counting defaults; only Sequence.\`Protocol\` is affected. RECOMMENDATION: Option (a) with refinement — relocate `var count` to Collection.\`Protocol\` with typed return Index<Element>.Count { borrowing get }; retain `count(where:) -> Cardinal` on Sequence.\`Protocol\` (no shadowing concern; method-vs-property identifier shape). Migration scope: 4–6 commits across swift-sequence-primitives + swift-collection-primitives + swift-buffer-primitives for Phases 1–3+5–6; Phase 4 (~36 Group A types acquire Collection.\`Protocol\`) bundles with the existing class-(c) follow-up. Composes with: collection-sequence-protocol-detachment.md v1.1.0 DECISION (consistent — Collection.Protocol is the multi-pass indexed protocol), count-direct-vs-fluent-and-hint-namespace.md v1.0.0 DECISION (stdlib-aligned ergonomics preserved and strengthened), sequence-protocol-surface-simplification.md v1.1.0 DECISION (preserved literally — Option a does not add a 7th protocol), HANDOFF-buffer-borrowing-count-sequence-detachment.md (sibling local fix + this structural fix cohere). Assessment-only; no source edits.

RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-22): Tier 2 cross-package research closing the Tagged-vs-Carrier question for `Affine.Discrete.Vector` representation. Triggered by principal direction 2026-05-22 reverting commit `8e01ee3` ("Extract Affine Carrier + Ordinal + Tagged sub-targets to sibling packages") with the assessment that Tagged extraction "doesn't make sense" alongside the suspicion that the "tagged approach is vestigial." Pre-Analysis Reframe per [RES-022] separates three readings of the vestigial suspicion: R1 Tagged-as-package-extraction (already correctly reverted), R2 Tagged-as-representation-of-Vector (Vector reduced to `Tagged<Vector.Tag, Int>` alias — substantive question), R3 Tagged-as-generic-instantiator (Tagged generic could be retired). Current state verified 2026-05-22 against swift-affine-primitives Sources/: Affine.Discrete.Vector is a primitive struct (Affine.Discrete.Vector.swift:37-46) conforming to Carrier.`Protocol` as trivial-self-carrier (Affine.Discrete.Vector+Carrier.swift:11-18, post-Phase-2b — legacy `Affine.Discrete.Vector.\`Protocol\`` removed). Tagged: Carrier.`Protocol` with `Domain = Tag, Underlying = Underlying` (immediate, no cascade) per Tagged+Carrier.Protocol.swift:42-67. Five distinct Tagged roles in Affine audited and confirmed load-bearing: phantom-Tag-as-Domain, generic-instantiator across ~80 ecosystem aliases, Offset typealias on Tagged-of-Ordinal (Tagged+Affine.swift:33-62), cross-Tag Ratio scaling (Tagged+Affine.swift:248-291), cross-Tagged Ordinal-Vector retreat/displacement (Tagged+Affine.swift:168-235). R2 (Vector-as-Tagged-alias) REJECTED on five structural losses: [API-NAME-003] spec-mirroring identity, [IMPL-001] domain erasure (`Tagged<Vector.Tag, Int>` exposes Int unwrap, losing Vector arithmetic identity), Tagged-cascade asymmetry (nested Tagged loses immediate-Underlying), Ratio type-signature legibility regression, [RES-018] no second consumer. Same structural blockers that cancelled Decimal Phase 4 Tagged refactor (decimal-carrier-integration.md RECOMMENDATION 2026-04-26). R3 (Tagged generic retirement) REJECTED — Tagged is irreducible as the phantom-typed Carrier instantiator across ~80 Tagged-aliased sites and 18-package Vector consumer fan-out. R1 already disposed by the 2026-05-22 revert (commit 4a909e8). RECOMMENDATION: Carrier-based status quo — Vector remains a standalone struct; Tagged<Tag, Vector> remains the phantom-typed-domain instantiation; Affine Tagged + Carrier sub-namespace targets stay inside swift-affine-primitives. No representation change. Reframes the question: Tagged and Carrier are not competing representations — Carrier is the protocol abstraction, Tagged is one of two concrete struct implementations (the other being trivial-self-carriers like Vector itself). Five follow-ups surfaced: cosmetic Tagged→Carrier operator lifting audit (composes with affine-operator-unification-completeness.md DEFERRED), Memory.Address.Offset spelling verification, capability-lift-pattern.md v1.1.0 ecosystem-instances amendment (Affine.Discrete.Vector as fourth canonical adopter), Decimal Phase 4 cancellation rationale re-statement under [RES-018] amended case (b), Tagged-vs-Property carrier overlap.

Placement strategy for the llms.txt / llms-full.txt / LLMS.md AI-discoverability conventions across the Swift Institute ecosystem (60+ packages, 17 orgs, swift-institute.org website). Five options analyzed (website-only, repo-only, path-scoped, layered-selective, layered-eager) against three consumer surfaces (web search, code agents, training crawlers). Verifies primary-source claims about the spec status: llms.txt active proposal at llmstxt.org, llms-full.txt is community convention NOT in spec, LLMS.md is draft v0.1, GitHub Issue #18 (multi-property orgs) has no maintainer resolution, retrieval-time crawler behavior is opaque on the consuming side. Recommends Option D — author swift-institute.org/llms.txt + llms-full.txt now (routine repo work, push gated separately per feedback_blog_publish_two_steps), defer per-repo LLMS.md until LLMS.md hits 1.0 OR 2+ major code-agent vendors document fetching it ([RES-018] two-consumers hurdle applied to convention adoption). Updates release-readiness skill with [RELEASE-AI-DISC] one-line append per launch. Re-evaluation trigger: 2026-11-01 or first confirmed vendor disclosure.
documents.statusDetail: Recommendation: Option D layered-selective. Concrete actions enumerated; none authorized yet pending per-action go-ahead. Re-evaluate at 2026-11-01 or on first vendor disclosure of llms.txt fetching / LLMS.md spec 1.0, whichever fires first.

documents.topic: Apple swift-http-api-proposal: ownership and concurrency patterns (Tier 3). Five patterns catalogued with source excerpts: `consuming sending` on closure parameters, `~Copyable & ~Escapable` middleware, AsyncWriter providing OutputSpan, `withClient` scoped resource, SendableMetatype usage.

Apple HTTP middleware chain isolation (Tier 3) — ownership transfer (`consuming Input`) replaces shared mutable context as the synchronization mechanism for `~Copyable & ~Escapable` middleware values.
Apple HTTP AsyncWriter OutputSpan pattern (Tier 3) — writer hands out `inout OutputSpan<WriteElement>` for zero-copy append, replacing caller-allocates-then-writer-copies.
Apple HTTP `DefaultHTTPClient.withClient` scoped pattern (Tier 3) — combines typed throws, `~Copyable` return, and `borrowing` access for resources requiring cleanup.

ANALYSIS (v1.0.0, 2026-05-31): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. ROUND 1 deep-dive companion to apple-swiftlang-memory-buffer-allocator-survey — HOW apple/swiftlang IMPLEMENT arena/pool/bump/slab/stack allocator families (external-only; Round 2 pending). 9 types read verbatim with file:line: swift-syntax BumpPtrAllocator/RawSyntaxArena (slab-list bump, free-all-on-deinit, geometric x2-per-128-slabs growth, nodes hold unowned arena ref + root strong, cross-arena childRefs); sourcekit-lsp UnsafeStackAllocator/UnsafeStackArray (8KB inline tuple buffer, 64B pages, strict LIFO pop asserted, heap spill, no growth); runtime Concurrency StackAllocator/swift_task_alloc (task-local slab bump, strict LIFO fatal-on-violate, 1KB-bucket slabs, initial ~512B slab embedded in AsyncTask, no sync); runtime MetadataAllocator/PoolRange (single global lock-free double-word CAS bump, 64KB static BSS seed + 16KB pages, IMMORTAL never-freed/no-op-dealloc); compiler AllocationArena Permanent(immortal, ASTAllocated deletes operator delete)/ConstraintSolver(RAII-scoped, reset per type-check solve) over LLVM BumpPtrAllocator + USRBasedTypeArena; swift-java SwiftArena/AllocatingSwiftArena (lifetime-SCOPE not byte-allocator; FFM value-witness-table size/stride/destroy; confined AtomicInteger CAS vs auto GC Cleaner; composes JDK Arena.ofConfined/ofAuto; 2026-05-20 PR#761 removed per-instance AtomicBoolean); NIO ByteBufferAllocator(4-fn malloc vtable + custom-closure PR#3526)+_Storage(final-class CoW next-pow2)+AdaptiveRecvByteBufferAllocator(arithmetic x2/div2 NOT Netty table; eager-grow/hysteretic-shrink)+NIOPooledRecvBufferAllocator(whole-ByteBuffer recycler, uniquely-referenced reuse)+Pool<Element>(object free-list); stdlib withUnsafeTemporaryAllocation (flat 1024B threshold, runtime hook DEAD return-false/#if0, Builtin.stackAlloc+heap fallback)+SE-0524 OutputSpan wrapper. CROSS-CUTTING PATTERNS: slab-list bump dominant; free discipline splits immortal/strict-LIFO/CoW-pool by content lifetime; thread-safety=single-threaded-by-construction EXCEPT metadata lock-free CAS; alignment bounded/punted never generalized; universal heap fallback for oversized; 'arena' overloaded = memory-region vs lifetime-scope (swift-java separates); 8 of 9 are SPI/package/internal/Java -- NO public allocator/arena abstraction (implementation-level corroboration of survey section 10). Agents corrected author premises (swift-java AtomicBoolean-not-AtomicInteger; NIO arithmetic-not-table; no _fixLifetime; dead runtime hook). Round-2 axes: shape/free-discipline/sync/public-vs-hidden/alignment/value-facade-CoW.

documents.displayName: Apple / Swiftlang Memory, Buffer, and Allocator Survey
documents.topic: ANALYSIS (v1.0.0, 2026-05-31): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. ROUND 1 of 2 — pure EXTERNAL survey of apple/swiftlang's own approach to memory, buffers, and allocators (NO Institute comparison or gap-classification by design; Round 2 comparative COMPLETE -> memory-buffer-allocator-institute-vs-apple-comparative.md). Center of gravity is the Swift 6.2 Span family (Span/RawSpan/MutableSpan/MutableRawSpan/OutputSpan/OutputRawSpan/UTF8Span, SE-0447/0456/0464/0465/0467/0485) built on the ~Copyable/~Escapable/lifetime-dependency substrate (SE-0390/0446 + experimental @lifetime): safe NON-OWNING bounds- and lifetime-checked VIEWS that replace Unsafe*BufferPointer, at zero runtime cost. Read/mutate/construct = three types (Copyable vs ~Copyable enforces exclusivity). Fixed-size: InlineArray + integer generics + [N of T] sugar (SE-0452/0453/0483). VERIFIED HEADLINE: NO user-facing custom-allocator facility exists in the stdlib and none is accepted/pitched — SE-0454 is toolchain-mimalloc only; allocation control = unsafe pointers + withUnsafeTemporaryAllocation/withTemporaryAllocation (SE-0322/0524) + per-library types (NIO ByteBufferAllocator, swift-system _RawBuffer, swift-java FFM arenas SwiftArena/AllocatingSwiftArena, Foundation __DataStorage). Recurring storage idiom: value facade + final-class CoW + isKnownUniquelyReferenced + slice bookkeeping, Span on top. Foundation Data: #if DATA_LEGACY_ABI fork — legacy enum w/ inline <=14B optimization is DEFAULT on Apple platforms (ABI compat), new heap-only struct only on non-Apple. Constrained track: Embedded Swift two-tier allocating/non-allocating (platform-owned heap, stripped ARC no weak/unowned), swift-mmio zero-alloc typed registers. Policy layer: memory-safety vision + SE-0458 opt-in strict mode. CAVEAT: shipped on un-reviewed experimental @lifetime; SE-0516 BorrowingSequence Returned-for-revision yet present in source.
documents.statusDetail: Round 1 of 2 (external apple/swiftlang survey); Round 2 comparative COMPLETE (memory-buffer-allocator-institute-vs-apple-comparative.md). No Institute comparison in this doc by design.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-16): Tier 2 cross-package structural-shape decision for ASCII.Code (the renamed ASCII.Byte) as it joins the Byte.Protocol capability-marker family. Naming pre-cemented to ASCII.Code per [API-NAME-003] specification-mirroring (INCITS 4-1986 'code'/'code point' terminology) — not re-litigated. Three shapes evaluated per [RES-029] semantic-identity-first framing: (a) standalone `struct ASCII.Code` wrapping `UInt8` + one-line Byte.Protocol conformance with `rawValue → underlying` rename folded in; (b) `typealias ASCII.Code = Tagged<ASCII, Byte>` with classification methods attached via `extension Tagged where Tag == ASCII, Underlying == Byte` (Memory.Address pattern); (c) standalone `struct ASCII.Code` wrapping the institute `Byte` (not `UInt8`). RECOMMEND (a) on three tiers: (Tier 1 semantic identity, dispositive) Byte.Protocol.swift:63–66 + byte-protocol-capability-marker.md §Conformers name ASCII.Code as a peer byte-domain conformer alongside Byte itself, parallel to future Latin1.Byte / UTF8.Code_Unit / RFC-specific byte types — not as a Tagged-phantom-wrapping; INCITS 4-1986 'code' identity does not encode a phantom-tag relationship. (Tier 2 adjacent-type consensus) Byte itself is `struct Byte { let underlying: UInt8 }` — direct UInt8 wrap + direct Byte.Protocol conformance; ASCII.Code as the next family member should take the same shape. (Tier 3 tiebreakers among structurally-defensible options) Shape (a) has one-step storage indirection vs (b)/(c)'s two-step; `extension ASCII.Code` reads naturally vs (b)'s `extension Tagged where Tag == ASCII, Underlying == Byte`; future `Tagged<X, ASCII.Code>` composition works naturally under (a) via the recursive `Tagged: Byte.Protocol where Underlying: Byte.Protocol` extension, but under (b) requires `Tagged<X, Tagged<ASCII, Byte>>` which protocol-abstraction-for-phantom-typed-wrappers.md §'No deep lifting' explicitly does NOT support. Shape (c) dominated by (a) on every axis. Verified inherited state from prior subagent (do NOT re-derive): ZERO workspace files use ASCII.Byte as a typed value; consumer surface = 36 static `ASCII.Byte.<const>` accesses in 4 terminal-primitives files (constants typed UInt8 deliberately for Collection<UInt8> comparison) + 1 rawValue access in swift-foundations/swift-ascii's ASCII.Byte+INCITS_4_1986.swift + 283 ephemeral `.ascii.X` accessor sites (constructed-on-demand, consumed-immediately, never stored — these recompile against the renamed type with ZERO source changes). Migration sketch: add `.package(path: "../swift-byte-primitives")` dep (transitively pulls carrier + tagged); rename ASCII.Byte → ASCII.Code in 4 source files; mechanical rename at 36 const sites + 1 typed reference + DocC. The deferred `rawValue → underlying` rename per swift-ascii-primitives/Research/ascii-primitives-rawvalue-underlying-rename.md retires IMPLEMENTED in the same commit (rename + reshape land together). [RES-018] case (b) carve-out — domain-owned vocabulary at L1, not gated. Default mode advisory; principal authorizes implementation separately.
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0 (2026-05-16). Tier 2 cross-package per [RES-020] (cross-package consumers across swift-terminal-primitives + swift-foundations/swift-ascii + ephemeral-accessor consumers in swift-json, swift-standards stack, etc.; informal semantic commitment; medium cost of error reversible; several-releases lifetime). Discharges the structural-shape question for ASCII.Code as Byte.Protocol's next per-domain conformer. Predecessor arcs cited as constraints (do NOT re-litigate): byte-primitive-extraction-and-domain-naming.md v1.0.1 DECISION (cements Byte.Protocol; identifies ASCII.Code among expected conformers); byte-protocol-capability-marker.md v1.1.0 RECOMMENDATION (per-domain manual recipe is canonical; §1.2.1 recursion-vs-refinement constraint; ASCII.Code named alongside Byte in Conformers list); protocol-abstraction-for-phantom-typed-wrappers.md v1.4.0 IMPLEMENTED (per-type X.Protocol recipe; §'No deep lifting' constraint on nested Tagged relevant to shape (b)); ascii-primitives-rawvalue-underlying-rename.md (per-package, 2026-05-03 — pre-authorizes the rawValue rename, retires in same commit if (a) or (c) wins). Live-fire precedents cited: Byte (struct wrapping UInt8 + direct Byte.Protocol conformance — match for shape a); Memory.Address = Tagged<Memory, Ordinal> + Index<Element> = Tagged<Element, Ordinal> (typealias-over-Tagged precedents for shape b's pattern, available-but-not-mandated). Four open follow-ups: (1) recipe re-use confirmation for Latin1.Byte / UTF8.Code_Unit — handled by existing recipe, no new research; (2) constants typing UInt8-vs-ASCII.Code status quo preserved (out of scope); (3) hexValue/digitValue compound-name debt flagged for separate code-surface pass; (4) [API-NAME-001c] skill-rule promotion deferred to skill-lifecycle workflow. Per [RES-022] structural correctness drove tier-1; per [RES-029] semantic identity (IS-A) prioritized over cost; per [HANDOFF-013]/[HANDOFF-050] prior research + workspace-wide grep cited not re-derived.

documents.topic: Can Async.Mutex adopt @_rawLayout for inline storage like stdlib Mutex? Tier 2.

Tier 2 cross-package recommendation closing the L1 layer violation flagged in swift-async-primitives' 0.1.0 brief Phase 1 §#4. Investigation Q1 finding (verified 2026-05-04): the Kernel_Thread_Primitives fallback at Async.Mutex.swift:149-157 is unreachable. Four independent lines of evidence converge: (A) the precise module name Kernel_Thread_Primitives exists nowhere in the ecosystem — thread-mutex types DO exist at L2 (ISO_9945_Kernel_Thread, final class with manual lock/unlock) and L3 (Thread_Synchronization in swift-foundations/swift-threads, Kernel_Thread in swift-foundations/swift-kernel), but those are different module identifiers and incompatible API shapes (class with manual locking vs struct with withLock closure); (B) Synchronization is unconditionally available on every host that can build the package given Swift 6.3.1 toolchain floor + Swift 6 language mode + .v26 platforms — branch 2 covers all non-Apple, non-embedded targets; (C) zero callers, zero test coverage, undocumented in the package's DocC Semantics.md contract; (D) every in-package Async.Mutex consumer (Promise, Completion, Broadcast, Channel.Bounded, Channel.Unbounded, Publication, Semaphore via dependency, Waiter via Atomic) directly imports Synchronization separately for Atomic<T> state machines — the fallback is architecturally incoherent because a hypothetical Synchronization-less target would compile branch 3 and then fail every other consumer. Recommendation: Option 1 — single-commit deletion of lines 149-158 + cosmetic Kernel.Handoff.Cell doc-comment reference at Async.Publication.swift:63 + README §41 swift-kernel-primitives dep-list entry. Four rejected alternatives enumerated: Option 2 wire as real dep (creates a layer violation), Option 3 relocate to L3 (Q3+ path, not Q1's stop-condition), Option 4 leave alone (does not close brief §#4), Option 5 retarget at swift-threads/ISO_9945_Kernel_Thread (still a layer violation AND breaks the Mutex API surface — class-with-manual-locking vs struct-with-withLock). Stop-condition compliance per the handoff's Q1-vestigial branch: Q2/Q3/Q4 deliberately not investigated; the 4 L1 consumers (cache, pool, render, test) require no migration. Secondary observation tracked separately: surviving Darwin os_unfair_lock branch is platform-conditional code at L1 and would be a [PLAT-ARCH-008c] concern under strict reading; out of scope for Option 1 per handoff's secondary-concern framing. Execution remains gated per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes.
documents.statusDetail: v1.1.0. Recommendation: Option 1 — single-commit deletion of the vestigial Kernel_Thread_Primitives branch (Async.Mutex.swift:149-158) + cosmetic Kernel.Handoff.Cell doc reference (Async.Publication.swift:63) + README §41 spurious dep entry. Bundleable as one commit. Closes brief #4 BLOCKED ON INVESTIGATION → RESOLVED. Q2/Q3/Q4 deliberately not investigated per stop-condition. No consumer migration required. v1.1.0 refinement (2026-05-04): Evidence A precise framing (the module identifier — not the concept — is what doesn't exist), Evidence D added (cross-file Synchronization-import invariant), Option 5 added and rejected (retargeting at swift-threads or ISO_9945_Kernel_Thread; both are layer violations AND API-incompatible). Secondary observation (surviving Darwin os_unfair_lock branch as [PLAT-ARCH-008c] candidate cleanup) tracked as separable follow-up; not bundled into 0.1.0 closure since [PLAT-ARCH-008c]'s strengthened reading was scoped to named types (Kernel.Descriptor, Kernel.Process.ID, Kernel.Directory.Entry), and Async.Mutex is a forward-looking ecosystem cleanup item rather than a 0.1.0 tag blocker. Execution awaits explicit principal authorization.

Architecture for a coherent binary→text encoding family (base16, base32, base58, base62, base64, base85) replacing the legacy `swift-base62-primitives` (heritage drift from a renamed swift-standards package; mis-classified as tier 0 by the compute-tiers script when it actually has tier-14 deps; convention-mismatched namespace `Base62_Primitives`; only baseN package in the ecosystem). The radix axis is closed by encoding mathematics (~6 useful values capped by ~95 printable ASCII characters); the alphabet axis is open (RFC + de facto + custom). Recommends Option D: closed-radix `Binary.Base.`N`` nested types per [PLAT-ARCH-008k] precedent (`Windows.`32``) + `Property<Tag, Base>` from swift-property-primitives for call-site dispatch via `callAsFunction` (default-alphabet path) and per-Tag method extensions (variant alphabets). Aligns with [API-NAME-008] (multi-form operations under one root MUST use Property.View nested accessors) — `encode` is the root, alphabets are sub-operations. PHASE 2 SUPERSEDED by binary-base-n-rfc-4648-reconciliation.md (2026-05-07): the original's `[Verified] no rfc-4648 repo` claim was wrong; the canonical institute pattern (L1 primitives, L2 spec-literal delegating to L1, L3 unifier for envisioned public API) places the Property-extension public API in a future L3 unifier, not in L2 swift-rfc-4648. Phase 1 (swift-binary-base-primitives shipped 2026-05-07) stands; Phase 2 revised to refactor (not author) swift-rfc-4648; Phase 4 added for the L3 unifier. Empirical basis: experiment `binary-base-n-poc` V1-V7 (CONFIRMED 2026-05-07).

documents.topic: Reconciles `binary-base-n-encoding-family-architecture.md` v1.0.0 with two corrections that surfaced during Phase 2 execution: (1) `swift-ietf/swift-rfc-4648` already exists and is public — actively developed with `RFC_4648.Base{16,32,64}` types, RFC_4648.Base32.Hex / RFC_4648.Base64.URL sub-namespaces, String / [UInt8] extension inits per consumer ergonomics — the original doc's `[Verified: 2026-05-07] no rfc-4648 repo` claim was wrong (per [RES-023]); (2) user-clarified architectural principle: standards encode the spec literally and USE primitives as building blocks; primitives don't add to standards. Original Phase 2 had RFC alphabets as Property extensions on swift-binary-base-primitives's Encode tag — that inverts the dependency direction. Reconciliation applies the canonical institute three-tier pattern (swift-ascii-primitives L1 → swift-incits-4-1986 L2 → swift-ascii L3): swift-binary-base-primitives owns the closed-radix mechanism + parameterized algorithms; swift-rfc-4648 keeps its spec-literal RFC_4648.Base16/etc. surface and DELEGATES algorithm execution to the L1 primitive; the 'envisioned base public API' (`Binary.Base.`N`.encode(bytes)` with spec-default alphabets) lives in a future L3 unifier `swift-foundations/swift-binary-base` that composes L1 mechanism + L2 specs. Phase 2 revised to refactor (not author) swift-rfc-4648, gated on L1's public release. Phase 4 added for the L3 unifier.

documents.topic: IMPLEMENTED (v3.0.0, 2026-05-20): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide foundational analysis of the relationship between Bit, Byte, and Binary as namespace domains across the nine packages in the byte-ecosystem (swift-bit-primitives, swift-byte-primitives, swift-byte-parser-primitives, swift-byte-serializer-primitives, swift-binary-primitives, swift-binary-parser-primitives, swift-binary-coder-primitives, swift-binary-base-primitives, swift-binary-leb128-primitives). Triggered by principal's 2026-05-20 question on whether `Binary` is justified as a namespace or should collapse to a typealias for `Collection<Byte>`, and whether binary-parser-primitives + binary-serializer-primitives are redundant with byte-parser-primitives + byte-serializer-primitives. Formal three-layer stratification established: Bit (atomic information, GF(2) two-element field) → Byte (value-type, Bit^8 with byte-domain identity per byte-protocol-capability-marker.md, sibling to UInt8) → Binary (representation domain, the category of byte-sequence-based encoding morphisms — endianness, position, pattern, mask, cursor, serializable, parseable, coder, format, base-N, LEB128). Tier 3 SLR with parallel subagent verification of Rust ([bytes, byteorder, bitvec, nom, serde+bincode/postcard, leb128, deku] — no ecosystem collapses 'binary' to 'byte collection'; Rust distributes across bytes+byteorder+nom+format-crates), Haskell ([Word8, Data.Bits, ByteString, binary, cereal, attoparsec] — load-bearing finding: ByteString.Builder.Prim owns LE/BE primitives INSIDE storage package, refuting 'ByteString = [Word8]'; attoparsec ≠ binary.Get split runs along endianness-in-scope axis), and Go+OCaml (Go encoding/binary as peer-of-text encoding namespace; OCaml has no top-level Binary, uses Cstruct.BE/LE sub-modules). Five options evaluated: A status quo (ACCEPT — matches formal semantics + institute conventions); B collapse to typealias (REJECT — categorically wrong, no prior art, loses load-bearing structure, reverses W2 cascade); C collapse binary-parser into byte-parser (REJECT — violates [MOD-DOMAIN], contradicts Haskell attoparsec/binary.Get precedent, breaks dependency layering); D split Binary.Serializable per binary-primitives-package-decomposition.md (REAFFIRM existing RECOMMENDATION); E Tier 3 cursor-abstractions successor arc (REAFFIRM in-flight arc, do not pre-empt). [RES-023] verification: 14 empirical claims about package state confirmed file:line; binary-parser-primitives Package.swift line 62-63 verified depending on swift-byte-primitives + swift-byte-parser-primitives (binary-parser IS already built on byte-parser). [RES-021] contextualization step closes the 'binary = byte collection' reading against all four surveyed ecosystems. [RES-024] formal semantics: Binary is a category (objects: encoded representations; morphisms: serialize/parse/code/format; parameterizing structure: endianness/position/pattern/mask/cursor), NOT a set/collection/typealias. Why Binary ≠ Collection<Byte>: Binary.Endianness has no meaning on a raw byte collection (parameterizes encoding morphism, not collection); Binary.Position is Tagged<Byte, Ordinal> not Collection<Byte>.Index; Binary.Serializable maps T → [Byte] for arbitrary T (UInt32, JWT, IPv4.Address), conformance is on T not on [Byte]; under typealias the protocol would only conform to byte-collection types — empty content. Canonical organization table for all nine packages stamped with substrate dependencies. Composes with: byte-cursor-primitive-unification.md v1.3.0 IN_PROGRESS (does NOT pre-determine the cursor-abstractions successor arc's outcome); binary-primitives-package-decomposition.md (REAFFIRMS its split recommendations); transformation-domain-architecture.md v3.4.0 (inherits the Parser/Serializer/Coder/Formatter capability-package discipline); byte-discipline skill (composes with [API-BYTE-001..007]).
documents.statusDetail: v3.0.0 (2026-05-20). Tier 3 ecosystem-wide per [RES-020] — precedent-setting (governs the relationship between Bit/Byte/Binary as semantic domain layers; informs every future byte-ecosystem package and every binary-encoding consumer); cost-of-error very high (collapsing the three-layer stratification is one-way structural commitment that cascades through 9 primitives packages + 20+ L2 spec packages + every L3 composition consumer); expected lifetime timeless. Per [RES-019] internal grep first: 26 ecosystem-wide research docs surveyed; load-bearing predecessors cited not re-derived (transformation-domain-architecture.md DECISION, byte-protocol-capability-marker.md Tier 3 RECOMMENDATION, byte-arithmetic-conformance.md RECOMMENDATION ζ, binary-primitives-package-decomposition.md RECOMMENDATION, byte-cursor-primitive-unification.md IN_PROGRESS, broader-l2-l3-byte-typing-gap-plan.md IN_PROGRESS). Per [RES-021] parallel subagent verification with three independent surveys (Rust / Haskell / Go+OCaml), all primary-source URLs verified at write-time. Per [RES-023] empirical state of all nine packages verified file:line; binary-parser → byte-parser dependency confirmed in Package.swift; Binary.Serializable.Buffer.Element == Byte confirmed post-W2 retype. Per [RES-024] formal three-layer categorical semantics included inline (Bit as GF(2), Byte as Bit^8 with byte-domain identity, Binary as category of encoding morphisms). Per [RES-029] semantic-identity-first ranking: Tier 1 (categorical layer distinction Bit/Byte/Binary) dispositive; Tier 2 (adjacent-type consensus across Rust/Haskell/Go/OCaml — no ecosystem collapses binary to byte collection) confirms. Per [RES-022] structural correctness drove ACCEPT verdict on status quo + REAFFIRM on existing in-flight arcs; cost arguments engaged only as tiebreakers within structurally-defensible options. Closes Q1/Q2/Q3 (Binary as namespace, Binary as category, Binary ≠ Collection<Byte>); Q4/Q5 (binary-parser justification + already-builds-on-byte-parser); Q6/Q7 (three-layer model + canonical organization table for nine packages). Opens skill-promotion candidate for binary-discipline (three-layer model worth promoting to a sibling of byte-discipline).

documents.topic: Per-candidate analysis of splitting `swift-binary-primitives` sub-products into sibling L1 packages on the swift-binary-base-primitives precedent established earlier today (2026-05-07). Four candidates evaluated with both-sides argument per [RES-009]: (1) LEB128 → `swift-binary-leb128-primitives`; (2) Format → `swift-binary-formatter-primitives` (rename signal flagged); (3) Serializable → `swift-binary-serializer-primitives` (protocol-to-witness shape shift); (4) new `swift-binary-coder-primitives`. Empirical [RES-023] verification surfaced that `Binary.Coder<Output>` ALREADY EXISTS at swift-binary-parser-primitives/Sources/Binary Coder Primitives/ (plain witness conforming to canonical Coder.Protocol) — handoff's 'new package' framing is corrected to 'extraction candidate', and the architectural fit (decode signature is parameterized over Binary.Bytes.Input from parser substrate) makes extraction wrong. Verdicts: SPLIT LEB128 (second-consumer test passes via swift-binary-parser-primitives consuming Binary LEB128 Primitives as a Package.swift dep); HOLD Format/Formatter pending witness-shape resolution and upstream swift-format-primitives rename clarity; SPLIT-WITH-SHAPE-SHIFT Serializable→Serializer per canonical-witness-capability v1.2.0 DECISION + ascii-serialization-migration precedent (multi-phase, multi-org coordination, 9+ external consumers); DO NOT EXTRACT Coder (architectural fit wrong; existing placement correct). Property<Binary.Serialize, T> shape rejected for Serializer in favor of plain witness struct mirroring Binary.Coder<Output> — single-form operations don't warrant Property.View ceremony per [API-NAME-008]. No package creation in this session per the user's per-action authorization rule.
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0. Tier 2 cross-package decomposition analysis. Both-sides argued per the user's explicit instruction. Empirical state verified per [RES-023]: HEAD 55b6217, build clean, Binary.Coder pre-existence in swift-binary-parser-primitives, Binary.Serializable consumer surface (9+ packages across IETF/ISO/CPU/sockets/file-system/legal-pdf), Binary.LEB128 single-external-consumer state. Implementation gates: each subsequent step (package creation, consumer migration, deprecation) requires explicit per-action authorization per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes.md. 4 open questions surfaced for the principal.

documents.displayName: Broader L2/L3 Byte-Typing Gap — Plan + Per-Wave Outcomes
documents.topic: IN_PROGRESS (v1.0.0, 2026-05-19): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide investigation outcome + 6-wave execution plan for closing the L2/L3 byte-typing gap left after the 2026-05-19 ASCII-domain retyping arc. Investigates: how to migrate the ~6 remaining UInt8-substrate consumer packages (swift-iso-32000 PDF, swift-rfc-7519 JWT, swift-rfc-4648 Base64/32, swift-incits-4-1986, swift-foundations/swift-ascii UInt8+INCITS_4_1986 + Binary.ASCII.Base62, swift-ascii-serializer-primitives Binary.ASCII wrapper) to Byte substrate without triggering the `.underlying`-mass-patching failure mode that forced the 2026-05-19 Phase 3 wrapper-deletion revert (b41d6f5 on swift-ascii-primitives + 531aa36 on swift-rfc-7519). Decomposition into 6 independently-dispatchable waves at workspace root per [HANDOFF-007] program-shape exception: W1 BSLI follow-ups (Set<Byte> trim, firstIndex byte-subseq lift, value→ASCII serialize without String intermediate) | W2 Binary.Serializable parent protocol retype Buffer.Element UInt8 → Byte + 89 consumer references workspace-wide | W3 6 cohort packages' UInt8 container retype to Byte (parallel sub-agents per package) | W4 Phase 3 wrapper deletion (UInt8+ASCII.swift ONLY; keep Carrier.Protocol+ASCII.swift per principal direction) | W5 /promote-rule [PREFIX-NNN] forbid UInt8.ascii.X regression | W6 ~38 audit-marked sites → ASCII.Code.digitValue/.hexValue per byte-arithmetic-conformance.md RECOMMENDATION ζ. Hard rules across all waves: type-up at CONTAINER + CONFORMING-PROTOCOL level NOT call-sites; .underlying mass-patching is a stop-and-surface signal; .underlying carve-out only for genuine stdlib-interop boundaries; [HANDOFF-035] workspace-wide grep + ecosystem build gate at end of each wave. Open questions: swift-incits-4-1986 [UInt8]+INCITS_4_1986.ASCII.swift deliberate-namespace disposition (surface at W3); Binary.Parseable symmetric inclusion (W2 pre-flight); File.System Binary.Serializable extension treatment (W2 if compile-forced); W5 host skill identification (byte-protocol-capability-marker or sibling). Predecessors: HANDOFF-broader-l2-l3-byte-typing-gap-program.md (annotated superseded by overview + wave files), HANDOFF-byte-arithmetic-conformance.md (research COMPLETE per byte-arithmetic-conformance.md v1.0.0 RECOMMENDATION ζ; retire at next /reflect-session). documents.displayName: Bulk/Span Iteration: Fold-vs-Separate Under the Ownership Model
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-26): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide evidence survey for the in-progress Iterator.`Protocol` / sequencer end-state decision — should bulk/chunk (Span-based) iteration be FOLDED into the one iterator protocol as an optional defaulted fast-path, kept as a SEPARATE refining protocol, or routed ELSEWHERE? The crux: Span<Element> admits Element: ~Copyable but NOT ~Escapable (SE-0447, Span/Span.swift:25-29), so folding a Span-based bulk hook onto one iterator protocol forces the whole protocol Element to Escapable, destroying ~Escapable-element scalar iteration. Evidence (stdlib in-flight SE-0516 source on the swiftlang/swift clone: separate BorrowingIteratorProtocol/BorrowingSequence with Element: ~Copyable, adapter bridge, @reparentable + deferred reparenting; SE-0516 Alternatives-Considered EXPLICITLY rejects the scalar/folded next()->Borrow<Element> design on performance + call-site-ergonomics grounds; Forums Iterable revision; for-borrow/for-inout pitch) leans decisively SEPARATE. A 2026-05-26 /tmp spike proved FOLD compiles + dispatches but only with Element: ~Copyable (Escapable retained) — confirming the crux. Extends unified-iteration-design.md, unified-iteration-design.md (D-2), collection-sequence-protocol-detachment.md, iterator-span-buffer-elimination.md; supplies the missing SE-0516-vs-institute comparison. Principal decides; doc presents evidence + implications only.
documents.statusDetail: Evidence survey for the principal’s fold-vs-separate decision; not itself a decision.

documents.displayName: Byte Arithmetic Conformance — Lift, Target, or Stay
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-19): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide companion to byte-protocol-capability-marker.md v1.1.0. Settles Q3 (should Byte gain arithmetic operators?) structurally symmetric to the parent's Q1 (should UInt8 conform to Byte.Protocol?). Six options enumerated (α full BinaryInteger/Numeric, β Cardinal-shaped, γ Ordinal-shaped with Byte.Offset = Tagged<Byte, Affine.Discrete.Vector>, δ targeted operators only, ε BSLI helpers only, ζ status quo) and all five arithmetic options REJECTED. RECOMMEND ζ: Byte does NOT gain arithmetic; ASCII-arithmetic friction documented in swift-byte-primitives/Research/bsli-gap-inventory.md 'Open friction note: ASCII-arithmetic ergonomics' is resolved by migrating consumer sites to the ALREADY-EXISTING ASCII.Code API in swift-ascii-primitives — ASCII.Code wraps UInt8 (ASCII.Code.swift), conforms to Byte.Protocol (ASCII.Code+Byte.Protocol.swift), and exposes digitValue:UInt8?/hexValue:UInt8? (ASCII.Code+Parsing.swift) plus isDigit/isHexDigit/isLetter/isAlphanumeric/isUppercase/isLowercase/lowercased()/uppercased() (ASCII.Code+Classification.swift). The downstream arc is consumer-site migration, NOT type creation. Workspace-wide .underlying site inventory (114 categorizations / 86 unique lines): direct arithmetic beneficiaries = 3 production sites (2× C3 bounded-subtraction in Binary.ASCII.Parsing.Machine.Decimal.swift:58,119 + 1× C5 wrapping-subtraction in ASCII.Decimal.Parser.swift:43), all migrate to ASCII.Code.digitValue. C2 (pure byte+byte = byte) has ZERO sites in production; C7 (bit-field via .underlying) has ZERO sites (Byte.Protocol+Bitwise covers it); 73 [C1c] sites already absorbed by landed Integer+Byte.swift BSLI; 6 LEB128 C4 sites already covered by Byte.Protocol+Bitwise '&' + BSLI widening. Rationale: (1) Cardinal precedent — total + admissible because adding cardinalities IS a cardinality; Byte fails the structural-coherence test (Byte+Byte has no byte-domain result); (2) byte-vs-arithmetic separation must be preserved from both sides (Q1 protects UInt8 from byte-domain pollution; Q3 protects Byte from arithmetic-algebras pollution); (3) protocol-vs-type placement has no correct answer under any arithmetic option (Byte-only inconsistent; Byte.Protocol over-applies to ASCII.Code / future UTF8.Code_Unit conformers); (4) BSLI partition 100% coverage without Byte arithmetic — every site routes to landed BSLI, existing bitwise lifts, existing stdlib conformances, existing ASCII.Code API, future BSLI candidate, or PERMANENT bridge. Affine.Discrete.Ratio considered per principal hint: rules out γ — Ratios act on vectors/counts, not points (Affine.Discrete.Ratio.swift:36-39 documents 'Index<A> * Ratio<A,B> compile error'); Byte is not a position; ASCII-decimal-decode is affine subtraction not multiplicative scaling. Companion to byte-protocol-capability-marker.md (no supersession; symmetric Q3 verdict). Closes bsli-gap-inventory.md 'Open friction note: ASCII-arithmetic ergonomics' and 'ASCII.Classification predicate signatures' follow-up.
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0 (2026-05-19). Tier 3 ecosystem-wide per [RES-020] — precedent-setting (governs every future byte-shape capability marker: Char.Protocol, Codepoint.Protocol, Word.Protocol, Line.Protocol); cost-of-error very high (arithmetic on capability markers is a one-way structural commitment); expected lifetime timeless. Per [RES-021] contextualization step closes the apparent-gap reading of external prior art. Per [RES-022] structural correctness drove the recommendation; sites-eliminated tiebreaker confirms ζ. Per [RES-023] empirical .underlying inventory verified workspace-wide (114 site-categorizations / 86 unique lines / 3 direct arithmetic beneficiaries) AND empirical verification of ASCII.Code's live API surface (ASCII.Code exists, conforms to Byte.Protocol, has digitValue/hexValue/classification accessors in production). Per [RES-029] semantic-identity-first ranking: Tier 1 (Byte's intrinsic semantics rule out every algebraic shape) dispositive; Tier 2 (adjacent-type consensus — Cardinal/Ordinal/Vector + Q1 verdict on UInt8) confirms. Per [HANDOFF-013] / [RES-019] internal prior art grep done first. Companion (not supersession): byte-protocol-capability-marker.md v1.1.0. Downstream arc: ASCII.Code CONSUMER-MIGRATION arc in swift-ascii-primitives — 21 sites in 5 files (3 ASCII arithmetic + 17 ASCII.Classification predicate forwarding + 1 ASCII.Hexadecimal bound-then-switch); migration not type creation since ASCII.Code's full API is already live.

documents.topic: IN_PROGRESS (v1.3.0, 2026-05-17 downgrade): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide cursor unification analysis, status downgraded RECOMMENDATION → IN_PROGRESS after Phase 0 prior-research grep surfaced two load-bearing prior artifacts v1.3.0's analysis did not engage. v1.3.0 had recommended Shape F (unify Binary.Bytes.Input.View and Lexer.Scanner via decompose-compose-reuse — Cursor<DomainTag>: ~Copyable, ~Escapable with position: Tagged<DomainTag, Ordinal>); analysis was empirically grounded (Text.Position ≡ Tagged<Text, Ordinal> at Text.Position.swift:27, Index<Byte> ≡ Tagged<Byte, Ordinal> at Index.swift:38) and survives as INPUT to the Tier 3 successor arc on cursor abstractions across the institute L1 ecosystem (cursor-abstractions-l1-ecosystem.md v1.0.0 RECOMMENDATION 2026-05-17) — NOT as a decided answer here. The two missed artifacts engaged by the Tier 3 successor: (a) Binary.Cursor in swift-binary-primitives (Sources/Binary Cursor Primitives/Binary.Cursor.swift) — a third cursor abstraction (~Copyable & Escapable owned reader-writer over Storage: Memory.Contiguous.Protocol & ~Copyable where Storage.Element == UInt8, dual Index<Storage> reader/writer indices, throws Binary.Error move/set operations), structurally distinct from the two ~Copyable & ~Escapable Span-cursors v1.3.0 evaluated; (b) swift-binary-primitives/Research/Lifetime Dependent Borrowed Cursors.md (RECOMMENDATION, 2026-01-19, ~1200 lines) — prior research arguing protocol-based dispatch (Binary.Bytes.Parser) is structurally required for ~Escapable borrowed cursors under Swift 6.x, plus a Two Worlds architecture (owned Binary.Bytes.Input + Parsing.Parser vs borrowed Binary.Bytes.Input.View + Binary.Bytes.Parser) that v1.3.0's Shape F framing did not engage. v1.3.0's prior-research grep was ecosystem-wide only; the per-package grep that surfaced these ran AFTER v1.3.0 was authored — RECOMMENDATION status was structurally unreliable on incomplete analysis. SUPERSEDED as the canonical answer by cursor-abstractions-l1-ecosystem.md v1.0.0; Shape F decomposition insight retained there as INPUT to the Three-Worlds Architecture (Shape γ).

documents.topic: DECISION (v1.0.1, 2026-05-15): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide architectural decisions cemented from the 2026-05-15 byte extraction arc. Four decisions: (1) LargerDomain.Subdomain naming ordering — subject owns namespace when role/specialization at intersection; formalized across Byte × Parser and prior ASCII × Parser instances; promoted to code-surface [API-NAME-001b]. (2) Byte location split — Byte value type in swift-byte-primitives (zero parser deps); Byte.Input typealias in swift-byte-parser-primitives sibling L1 package. (3) Input is peer-foundational — deleted ~148 Parser.Input.* re-exports; consumers reference Input_Primitives.Input.X directly; verified 7 non-parser direct L1 consumers of swift-input-primitives (binary-coder, infinite, dictionary, list, queue, heap, terminal). (4) Byte.Protocol as refinement of Carrier.Protocol<UInt8> + stdlib basics (Sendable/Equatable/Hashable/Comparable/ExpressibleByIntegerLiteral); UInt8 conformance question deferred to capability-marker Tier 3 branching session. Plus Parser.Input qualification cost-IS-the-contract framing (extension Parser scopes now require Input_Primitives.Input.X qualification — verbose by design; surfaces architectural meaning syntactically). Binary-stack audit (binary-primitives, binary-parser-primitives, binary-base-primitives, binary-coder-primitives, binary-leb128-primitives) returned GREEN — no byte reimplementation across all five packages. documents.displayName: Byte.Protocol as Capability Marker — Generalization, UInt8 Conformance, and Meta-Pattern Status
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.1.0, 2026-05-15): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide research discharging the question deferred by byte-primitive-extraction-and-domain-naming.md v1.0.1 §Open Questions 4.B. Two sub-questions answered. Q1 (should UInt8 conform to Byte.Protocol?): NO — UInt8 MUST NOT conform; Byte.Protocol conformance restricted to institute byte-domain types (Byte, plus future ASCII.Code / Latin1.Byte / UTF8.Code_Unit / RFC-specific byte types) with recursive Tagged conformance via `extension Tagged: Byte.Protocol where Underlying: Byte.Protocol, Tag: ~Copyable`. Rationale: (a) semantic identity per [RES-029] — UInt8 is arithmetic-algebras carrier, Byte is byte-domain twin; (b) adjacent-type consensus — UInt does NOT conform to Cardinal.Protocol, Int does NOT conform to Affine.Discrete.Vector.Protocol; conforming UInt8 would be the first exception; (c) bundle-conflict cost — operator shadow on `<`/`==`/`hash`, API-surface broadening to all UInt8 sites, Carrier-composition pollution of Tagged<_, UInt8>; (d) author intent encoded inline at Byte.Protocol.swift file header. Q2 (is there a meta-pattern worth naming/promoting?): per-domain manual X.Protocol recipe IS the canonical pattern; promote to skill rule (proposed [API-NAME-001c] or capability-marker rule family) under code-surface; decline meta-protocol variant (blocked by [IMPL-102] — Swift overlapping-conformance rules force incomplete implementation; same verdict as capability-lift-pattern family) and generator-macro variant (hides §1.2.1's recursion-vs-refinement structural decision). v1.1.0 §1.2.1 names the recursion-vs-refinement constraint principle: refinement-of-Carrier X.Protocol blocks recursive Tagged conformance because Tagged<Tag, X>.Underlying == X (per Tagged's universal Carrier conformance), not the bottom-most carrier type. Sibling form is required when recursive Tagged conformance is needed or anticipated — true for every Group A capability marker. Tagged-recursive-conformance to Byte.Protocol landed v1.1.0 alongside the sibling-form refactor of Byte.Protocol; the refinement→sibling change preserves Q1+Q2 verdicts and flips only the Byte-specific sibling-vs-refinement judgment. Prior art surveyed: internal (property-tagged-semantic-roles Group A/B taxonomy and fibration framing; phantom-typed-value-wrappers-literature-study Tier 3 SLR; protocol-abstraction-for-phantom-typed-wrappers Tier 3 DECISION rejecting unified-protocol Option I; cardinal-protocol-unification-memo live-fire six-package precedent; carrier-walkback reflection establishing [IMPL-102]); external (Rust Book ch10-02/ch20-02 orphan rule + newtype pattern verified; HaskellWiki Newtype convention verified; Scala 3 given/using as scoped alternative; swiftlang/swift Integers.swift Numeric hierarchy verified).

RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-22): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide investigation resolving axis B (canonical-attachment semantic) deferred by sibling-refines-canonical-attachment.md v1.1.0 §10a. Recommends path (c) sanctioned-but-deferred: additive Common.Codable peer protocol alongside the existing canonical attachment trio (Coder_Primitives.Codable, Parser_Primitives_Core.Parseable, Serializer_Primitives_Core.Serializable). [FAM-002]'s type-commitment semantic is retained UNCHANGED for the existing trio; the new peer carries author-choice-with-fallback semantic for currency types (Range, CGRect, UUID, etc.) that have no single inherent canonical codec but do have a clean cross-format floor representation. New rule [FAM-011] codifies the design-space reservation; authoring is deferred until empirical pull (consumer-driven currency-type need OR Apple New Codable proposal reaches formal Swift Evolution pitch). Workspace-wide canonical-attachment conformer inventory per [HANDOFF-021] with live grep output and three-class classification: Class I spec-value-type commitments (RFC_8259.Value at JSON.Coder.swift:125 with [FAM-003] justification, Version.Semantic + Version.Tools + Version.Calendar + Glob.Pattern — exactly match [FAM-002] type-commitment); Class II generic-instantiated delegations (Tagged + Optional — semantically neutral lifting pattern); Class III legacy stdlib pins (10 FixedWidthInteger types — RECOMMENDED-FOR-MIGRATION per Φ.3, treated as defects). Apple New Codable live-state survey per [RES-031]: t/78585 178 posts last reply 2026-03-06; t/85186 71 posts last reply 2026-05-22 active. Prototype IS structurally path (c) — additive CommonEncodable/CommonDecodable peer alongside JSONEncodable/JSONDecodable siblings; Perry verbatim quote 'entirely distinct from the format-agnostic one' confirms peer relationship not refinement. Tipping-question enumeration (§4) settles NO: a sufficiently-rich canonical CANNOT absorb every format's native concerns without translation loss — categorical mismatches for protobuf field numbers + XML attribute-vs-element + plist Data/Date; format-shape-leak costs for CBOR tags + MessagePack ext types + JSON null-vs-missing-key. This rules out path (b)'s effective rich-canonical reframing; the canonical must be either narrow type-commitment (path a) or thin floor (path c new peer) — not a rich primary surface. Per-path mechanical analysis + per-rule interaction analysis ([FAM-001]–[FAM-010]) + cognitive-dimensions analysis (path c wins 3 dimensions, path a wins 1 viscosity, 1 tie). Path (c) sanctioned-but-deferred bounds the viscosity cost while preserving every existing FAM rule intact. Companion to sibling-refines-canonical-attachment.md v1.2.0 (amended same date with axis B Disposition updated to RESOLVED-VIA-PATH-(c)-DEFERRED), per-package family-codable-convention.md v1.1.4 ([FAM-001]–[FAM-008]), and ecosystem-wide 2026-05-15-family-codable-convention.md v1.0.0 ([FAM-009]).

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-05-23): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide cross-language survey answering whether the contiguous/linear discipline is the universal canonical default general-purpose growable buffer, and whether swift-buffer-primitives (now pure substrate after the 8-way discipline extraction to swift-buffer-{linear,ring,slab,linked,slots,arena,unbounded,aligned}-primitives) should designate a canonical default. Survey of 8 ecosystems against verified primary sources: Swift stdlib Array/ContiguousArray/ArraySlice (no stdlib Deque — deque is swift-collections-only); Rust Vec ('contiguous growable array type… (ptr,cap,len) triplet… as low-overhead as possible') vs VecDeque ('growable ring buffer', peer specialization); C++ std::vector under normative C++ Core Guidelines SL.con.2 ('Prefer using STL vector by default') vs non-contiguous std::deque; Java ArrayList (array-backed default) + ArrayDeque (circular-array deque peer, LinkedList deprecated-by-guidance); Go slice (contiguous language built-in, no std deque); Python list (contiguous dynamic array, over-allocated append) + collections.deque (block-linked, opt-in); C# List<T> (array-backed) + Queue<T> (circular array); Zig std.ArrayList ('contiguous, growable… similar to std::vector and Vec'). Finding: contiguous/linear is the canonical default in 8 of 8 — no exception; ring/deque is universally a named peer specialization (absent entirely from Swift stdlib + Go). Rationale for contiguity-as-default: cache locality / single-dereference random access (C++ SL.con.2 explicit), lowest space overhead ((ptr,cap,len) minimalism), amortized geometric-growth append, smallest mental model (fewest invariants), and lightest transitive dependency set. The dependency-weight axis bridges to the institute constraint: per [MOD-032] a canonical-in-owner discipline cannot depend on another buffer sibling (every sibling depends on the owner → cycle); verified per-sibling Package.swift — Unbounded→Aligned→owner is a cycle (disqualified), Linear depends only on owner targets + Storage.Heap + lower-tier primitives (cycle-safe). Three designation options analyzed: D1 re-absorb Linear into owner (cycle-safe but reverses the just-ratified pure-substrate identity, asymmetric), D2 umbrella @_exported re-export (DISQUALIFIED — forms owner⇄Linear [MOD-032] cycle), D3 documentation-only canonical (cycle-safe, identity-preserving, symmetric, reversible). [RES-029] semantic-identity-first: Tier-1 identity (owner = substrate post-extraction) dominates; the ergonomic 'import yields a buffer' benefit is already delivered one layer up by Array (= Buffer.Linear-backed per data-structures-linear-collections-assessment.md). RECOMMENDATION: yes, designate the contiguous/Linear discipline as canonical default, implemented as D3 (README/DocC guidance naming Buffer.Linear canonical + discipline-selection decision table) — NOT D1/D2. Re-absorb-vs-doc is a principal identity call (kept RECOMMENDATION not DECISION). Extends internal prior art: theoretical-buffer-primitives-design.md Tier 3 DEFERRED (Linear/Ring/Slab as the 3 fundamental disciplines), ecosystem-data-structures-inventory.md (Buffer.Linear = 'general-purpose contiguous buffer', backing Array/Stack/Heap/Dictionary.Ordered/Set.Ordered), comparative-buffer-primitives.md (flagged stale module map post-extraction). Read-only research dispatch — no .swift / Package.swift edits.

Per [RES-019] internal grep first applied across swift-institute/Research + swift-buffer-primitives/Research — load-bearing precedents cited (theoretical-buffer-primitives-design.md, ecosystem-data-structures-inventory.md, data-structures-linear-collections-assessment.md, comparative-buffer-primitives.md); internal position (Linear = general-purpose contiguous default) extended not re-derived. Per [RES-023] every empirical claim verified at write time: per-sibling Package.swift .package(path:) declarations read directly (the cycle table), owner substrate state (3 targets, no buffer-sibling deps) read from Package.swift + Sources/, Linear→Storage.Heap backing confirmed via target products + source grep. documents.topic: Tier 2 swift-primitives-scoped enforcement-rule design grounded in empirical baseline of current Sources/. Maps user directive 'move away from bitPattern and unchecked inits, stay high typed, prefer map and retag' onto the SwiftLint Tier 1/Tier 2 canonical configs landed in Phase 1.5 / Tier-A. Empirical baseline (parallel-subagent grep, file-level counts, methodology per [RES-023]): Int(bitPattern: 180 files (mixed: bare form is the [INFRA-002] integration overload; chained .rawValue form is the anti-pattern); __unchecked: 41 files (mixed: declaration-site legitimate, call-site anti-pattern); .rawValue. chains 12 files; count - 1 34 files; Cardinal(0)/(1) 2 files (almost clean); Swift.min(...rawValue) 0 / count -= 1 0 / count &-= 1 0 / position.rawValue + 0 (already-clean discipline). Cohort design recommends 4 LAND rules (R1 cardinal_count_minus_one_anti_pattern regex `\bcount\s*-\s*1\b` warning ~50-100 hits cite [INFRA-025]; R2 cardinal_zero_one_constructor_anti_pattern regex `\bCardinal\(\s*[01]\s*\)` warning 2 hits cite [INFRA-101]; R3 chained_rawvalue_access_anti_pattern regex `\.rawValue\.\w+` warning 12 hits cite [INFRA-103]; R4 bitpattern_rawvalue_chain_anti_pattern regex `Int\(bitPattern:\s*[^)]*\.rawValue` warning ~30-80 hits cite [INFRA-002]/[CONV-016]) all at Tier 2 (primitives-specific, mirrors no_foundation_import_*/l1_no_platform_conditionals architectural placement; would be wrong at Tier 1 because Cardinal/Tagged-rawValue are primitives-specific types). 2 DEFER rules to SwiftSyntax-based tooling (R5 __unchecked: at call sites only — needs call-site-vs-decl-site AST distinction; R0 positive enforcement — needs conversion-tier classification per ai-context-reduction-via-type-system-tooling.md Option D Symbol-graph pipeline). 3 SKIP rules (R6 bare Int(bitPattern:) IS the canonical [INFRA-002] integration overload; R7 Swift.min(rawValue) and R8 position.rawValue + arithmetic both 0 hits already clean). Total expected diagnostic surface on landing: ~80-130 violations concentrated in swift-buffer-primitives, swift-tree-primitives, swift-ordinal-primitives, swift-hash-table-primitives. Failing CI is the intended diagnostic per user posture established Phase 1. R5/R0 deferral is the architectural retreat parallel to API-IMPL-005's Phase 1 retreat — both require AST-level context unavailable to NSRegularExpression. Composability with existing Tier 2 (no_foundation_import_*, l1_no_platform_conditionals): all five primitives-specific Tier 2 rules share reach (132 sub-packages via parent_config:) and severity model (warning + --strict CI gating).
documents.statusDetail: v1.0.0. Tier 2 primitives-scoped enforcement-rule design closing the user directive 'stay high typed, prefer map and retag.' Empirical baseline grounded per [RES-023] (parallel-subagent grep against current swift-primitives Sources/, methodology documented). Recommendation: land 4 SwiftLint custom rules (R1-R4) in swift-primitives/.github/.swiftlint.yml as a single Tier 2 update commit; defer 2 (R5 __unchecked call-site, R0 positive-enforcement) to SwiftSyntax-based tooling Phase per ai-context-reduction-via-type-system-tooling.md Option D; skip 3 (R6 bare Int(bitPattern:) is canonical, R7/R8 already clean). Composes architecturally with the Phase 1.5 Tier 2 cohort (no_foundation_import_*, l1_no_platform_conditionals). No code change executed. Implementation gated per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes — next dispatch awaits authorization. Source-cleanup of the surfaced ~80-130 violations is a separate downstream Phase (parallel to Phase 2+ deferred work for [DOC-003], [PRIM-FOUND-001], [API-IMPL-005]).

documents.topic: Decision (Option C, 2026-05-04) to add Cardinal.`Protocol` upstream in swift-cardinal-primitives — sibling to Carrier (not refinement), mirroring the Ordinal.`Protocol` precedent — to unify bare `Cardinal` and `Tagged<Tag, Cardinal>` under one constraint. Resolves the bare-vs-Carrier overload split pattern observed across six packages (swift-ordinal-primitives, swift-affine-primitives, swift-cyclic-primitives, swift-sequence-primitives, swift-finite-primitives, swift-bit-vector-primitives) after the Tagged `46ded75` cascade-drop. The memo body originally proposed an empty-marker `Carrier.`Protocol` where Underlying == UInt` shape; correction at top: that does not compile because `Tagged<Tag, Cardinal>.Underlying == Cardinal` post-cascade-drop. The working shape is the sibling pattern with its own `var cardinal: Cardinal { get }` accessor. Vector.`Protocol` deferred to L3 evidence accumulation. NOTE: Pending supersession by `cardinal-trivial-self-revert-plan.md` (RECOMMENDATION 2026-05-04). The principal's stated direction post-DECISION reverses Option C in favor of trivial-self-Carrier on Cardinal/Ordinal/Vector with `Carrier<Cardinal>` as the universal unifier — eliminating the need for `Cardinal.`Protocol``. Status will move to SUPERSEDED once the revert plan is authorized for execution.
documents.statusDetail: SUPERSEDED 2026-05-04 by cardinal-trivial-self-revert-plan.md. Phase 5 verified green on 2026-05-04 with Option C build-blocker scope authorization: 10 L1 BLOCKERS (set, graph, pool, structured-queries, symmetry, test, heap, algebra-linear, observation, dictionary) + 1 L2 BLOCKER (color-standard) fixed using mechanical Finding A/B accessor adjustments; downstream cascade (async, dictionary) unblocked. Stylistic-only leftovers and newly-discovered cascade repos enumerated in the plan's `Deferred follow-up — Tagged-rename adoption sweep` section. The Cardinal.`Protocol` unification approach in this memo is preserved as historical context.

documents.topic: Per-repo revert plan (RECOMMENDATION, 2026-05-04) restoring trivial-self-Carrier semantics on Cardinal/Ordinal/Affine.Discrete.Vector across ~13 of 23 inspected swift-primitives repos, while preserving (a) Tagged's `46ded75` immediate-wrap, (b) the Carrier-protocol-level `rawValue` → `underlying` rename, and (c) all parallel CI/metadata/Phase-7a-revalidation commits. Reverses the cascade endpoint documented in cardinal-protocol-unification-memo.md (Option C). Post-revert design: bare Cardinal `Underlying == Cardinal` (trivial-self) + Tagged<Tag, Cardinal> `Underlying == Cardinal` (immediate-wrap) → `Carrier<Cardinal>` matches both as universal unifier; `Cardinal.`Protocol`` sibling deleted. Three mixed commits (`ac7f308` cardinal, `8e83f7f` ordinal, `24a9286` vector) addressed via Mechanism A (hand-authored partial-revert commits separating rename portion from collapse portion). 5 phases: (1) carrier DocC text edit, (2) cardinal/ordinal/affine partial-reverts, (3) consumer reverts in bit-vector/binary/bit/memory, (4) buffer-primitives single-file accessor adjustment, (5) workspace-wide grep + ecosystem-wide build gate per [HANDOFF-035]. Estimated diff ~535 lines across 13 repos. Open Questions: (#1) per-type machine-word accessor name under trivial-self — recommends `rawValue: UInt|Int` restoration; (#2) `Ordinal.`Protocol`` longer-term disposition (separate cycle); (#3) Vector arithmetic empirical verification; (#4) Tagged ~Copyable lifetime gap noted but out of scope. Pending principal authorization for execution.
documents.statusDetail: RECOMMENDATION 2026-05-04. Read-only drafting deliverable; execution gated on principal authorization of: (a) the revert plan as a whole, (b) Open Question #1 resolution (machine-word accessor name; recommends `rawValue`), (c) the supersession of cardinal-protocol-unification-memo.md. Per-phase per-repo execution is itself per-action authorization-gated per feedback_no_public_or_tag_without_explicit_yes (push gates remain separate from local commit gates).

documents.topic: Two-phase ecosystem inventory + triage for Carrier conformance, Tagged refactor, and `.Protocol` capability-lift adoption across swift-primitives (134) + swift-standards (21) + swift-foundations (137). Phase 1 inventory: 26 hoisted-protocol declarations classified into Carrier-shaped (4: Cardinal, Ordinal, Hash.Value, Affine.Discrete.Vector — last is NEW finding not in capability-lift-pattern.md ecosystem instances), witness-style (13, out per recommendation #6), abstraction-style (7, wrong shape), self-projection-default (1: Ownership.Borrow), pure marker (1: Witness); ~80 Tagged-aliased value carriers; ~7 standalone single-field wrappers; 1 strong ~Copyable Carrier candidate (Property<Tag, Base>). Phase 2 triage: CAN/SHOULD per candidate biased toward apply. Top-5 ranked: Decimal Tagged refactor / Clock.Offset+Nanoseconds .Protocol companions / Property Q2 Carrier conformance / Algebra.Modular.Modulus .Protocol / Ordinal.Finite refinement. Each becomes future per-package handoff dispatch.
documents.statusDetail: v1.1.0: Phase 1 (Tagged: Carrier) + Phase 2a/2b (Cardinal/Affine migrated; Ordinal.Protocol retained as sibling per Option G) + Phase 3 partial (Clock, Property: Carrier landed; Algebra.Modular.Modulus reclassified as RawRepresentable) + Phase 5 (Index.Bounded cascade ALREADY-MET) implementation log added. Phase 4 (Decimal Tagged refactor) pending.

documents.topic: Design choice for the public-init shape on Carrier (and Tagged via Carrier conformance) given the empirical evidence from the generic-throws-init experiment (swift-tagged-primitives/Experiments/generic-throws-init/, commits 2e0308c and 3686e7c). Two paths analyzed (path c — marker protocol — was rejected by user direction 2026-05-01): (a) keep the current __unchecked:-only discipline with consumers declaring per-domain inits via constrained extensions; (b) extend Carrier with V5 (default extension) or V6 (requirement + default impl) to provide a generic-throws init across all conformers. Decisive axis is validation-mandatory protection: V5/V6 add non-opt-out-able public surface (Swift offers no opt-out for default extensions or default-impl requirements); types whose semantics require validation-mandatory construction lose protection. Recommends path (a) on protection, reversibility, mid-launch-cohort discipline, and [RES-018] premature-primitive grounds.
documents.statusDetail: Recommendation: keep current __unchecked:-only discipline; no Carrier or Tagged source changes. Friction-reduction argument for V5/V6 is real but addresses only the wrapper-with-optional-validation case; consumers can handle this per-domain via V3-style overloaded extensions (the pattern the README already shows). Future revisit conditions documented (3+ packages re-deriving V3-style inits; a Swift language change lifting the H2 refutation; systematic __unchecked: misuse; validation-mandatory-type cases blocked from Carrier).

Tier 1 (6 items) gates the next package's audit; Tier 2 (11 items) covers process improvements; Tier 3 (10 items) opens research; Tier 4 (4 items) is consolidation.
