The HOW for the SE-0516 span-primitive Iterable realignment (the WHAT/WHY is iterable-se0516-alignment.md; this EXTENDS it per [HANDOFF-013]). Verify-plan subordinate output (Pattern B). (1) Re-verifies all 5 load-bearing claims from primary source (Iterable scalar-primitive Iterable.swift:45/Iterator.Protocol.swift:46; __IteratorChunkProtocol Escapable bound + scalar refinement __IteratorChunkProtocol.swift:34-35; Iterator.Chunk struct bound-free Iterator.Chunk.swift:26-27; Span<~Copyable> + borrowing addressor Span.swift:29/455-461; Memory.Contiguous.span ~Copyable-capable) + independent swiftlang/swift clone corroboration (BorrowingIteratorProtocol Element:~Copyable, nextSpan sole primitive, SpanIterator uses extracting on Span<~Copyable>) — NO claim refuted. (2) Ground-state: 4 packages at baseline SHAs (iterator b7277fb, memory-iterator 1c6d694, set-ordered 6af55a2, iterator-borrow dd60699), clean, NO drift. Precision note: D1 is a 4-part change (drop the : Iterator.Protocol refinement + add associatedtype Element/Failure, not just relax the bound). (3) 3-package reference-rework plan (D1→D6, per-package commits, the named build-verify gate Iterator.Chunk(span) over inline @_rawLayout for ~Copyable debug+release, SIL 0-witness_method gate, acceptance mapping). The : Iterable blast-radius enumeration (workspace-wide, [HANDOFF-050]/[HANDOFF-040] attribute-aware): 34 production conformers — 13 bridge-vended (Memory.Contiguous family: Set.Ordered/Buffer.Linear/Array/Memory.Contiguous) migrate FREE; 21 break under D2 (18 hand-rolled scalar: bit-vector x8, Vector x2, Buffer.{Linked,Linked.Inline,Slab.Inline,Ring.Inline}, Single, Input.Slice, Hash.Occupied.Static, Cyclic.Group.Static; + 3 ring piecewise) across 9 packages OUTSIDE the authorized scope — generators cannot project Span<Element>, inline @_rawLayout variants dodge the span demangle-crash. ESCALATION (Ground Rule 6a/6c FIRED): D2 is a workspace-wide breaking protocol change (mirror serves committed main); options A (atomic cascade), B (transition adapter), C (validate-in-isolation, defer D2 commit) — subordinate recommends C then A; supervisor decides scope/sequencing.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-02): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide / cross-layer. The unified model by which a type in the data-structure stack DERIVES AS MUCH API/BEHAVIOUR FOR FREE AS POSSIBLE by conforming to capability protocols -- Memory.Contiguous.Protocol -> Storage.Protocol -> Buffer.Protocol -> <ADT>.Protocol -- ALWAYS by COMPOSITION (a shared bridge / constrained extension keyed on the capability, vending free API to every conformer) and only rarely by REFINEMENT, refinement reserved for the warranted case. GENERALIZES cross-layer-capability-protocol-model.md (v1.3.0) from the single iteration+algebra concern to the WHOLE stack, and reconciles the CONTAINER tier (HAS-A composition chain) with the SCALAR tier ([API-NAME-001c] Per-Domain Capability-Marker Protocol; Group A markers Byte/Cardinal/Ordinal lift API via X.Protocol default impls + recursive Tagged forwarding) as TWO FACES OF ONE LAW -- both independently chose compose/relate-not-refine (the scalar tier for a MECHANICAL reason: refinement blocks recursive Tagged conformance, [IMPL-102]). FOUR PARTS: (1) per-protocol INVENTORY (REQUIRES/PROVIDES(D)/compose-bridges(C)/refines(R)) -- Memory.Contiguous.Protocol=span (zero D; the canonical memory->Iterable (C) bridge vends makeIterator->Iterator.Chunk(span) free, D4 ~Copyable-relaxed); Storage.Protocol={capacity,pointer(at:)} (zero D; no Storage-keyed span bridge); Buffer.Protocol=count (D: isEmpty; refines NOTHING); Iterable (D: forEach/contains/first/reduce); Collection.Protocol (REFINES Iterable -- the ONE sanctioned refine edge, landed 2026-06-01 commit 3cc21cc -- but carries NO makeIterator default). (2) the DERIVE-FOR-FREE MAP: free today (contiguous ADTs get iteration bridge-derived -- Array/Set.Ordered/Stack/Buffer.Linear have empty `: Iterable` bodies) vs hand-written (non-contiguous ADTs -- Queue ring / Dictionary slab -- correctly hand-write via Iterator.Materializing; derive-for-free is BOUNDED to capability-bearing conformers, 13-free/18-scalar/3-piecewise across 34 conformers). (3) the DECISION RULE + the four-part WARRANTED-REFINEMENT TEST (C1 identity AND C2 conformer-set AND C3 expressibility/recursion AND C4 cross-package-mechanics; all four must pass; the choice is FREE at the 0-witness specialization boundary so it is decided on semantics, not perf); Collection.Protocol: Iterable passes all four. (4) GAPS gated [DS-020]/[RES-018]: GAP-A (Array/Stack decline span-derived Equatable/Hashable that Set.Ordered takes over existing Span bridges -- the sharpest actionable, NO new primitive); GAP-B (Memory.Contiguous count/subscript (D) defaults); GAP-C (the §3.4 Storage->span bridge, two-part: add an init-count requirement first); GAP-E (drop redundant Buffer.Arena.isEmpty); GAP-F (a buffer-mutable core -- deferred-irreducible, gated); GAP-H (META: promote the container tier to a [PREFIX] rule, sibling of [API-NAME-001c]). SHARPEST QUESTION RESOLVED: Array's hand-written Collection witnesses are the DELIBERATE answer, NOT a bridge-gap -- Collection-ness decomposes into the ITERATION half (free: refine edge + memory->Iterable bridge over span) and the INDEX half (declared per-collection: typed Index identity + totality via .saturating()/.exact() which span cannot supply and non-contiguous collections also need); a memory->Collection bridge is possible-but-unwarranted (non-universal + low-value). The pair is exact: Array (contiguous; iteration bridge-derived over span) and Queue.DoubleEnded/Deque (swift-deque-primitives; non-contiguous ring; iteration via Iterator.Materializing) BOTH conform Collection (Bidirectional/Access.Random) and BOTH hand-write the same index witnesses -- index half declared per-collection, iteration half by whatever mechanism fits the storage. CRITICAL STANCE (§7.1): this doc evaluates the DESIGN and does NOT assume the current iteration/sequence/collection code is already correct/complete -- it flags a dormant NON-COMPILING Buffer.Linear: Collection.Protocol (lifetime-dependence error, GAP-I), a REDUNDANT Collection.Indexed whose stated reason-to-exist is stale (GAP-J -- VERIFIED 2026-06-02: DELETE; Collection.Protocol supports ~Copyable via _read + the D4 span bridge, Bidirectional refines Collection.Protocol not Indexed, all 6 Indexed conformers also conform Bidirectional so 0 orphaned, 0 consumers/refiners, swift-array-primitives builds green at HEAD; pure-subtraction cleanup, independent of GAP-I), demangle-crash-driven iterators (Buffer.Ring.Scalar / Dictionary pair-iterator), and stale stdlib-bridge docs (GAP-K). DESIGN/RATIONALE only -- no package edits.

IMPLEMENTATION STATUS (§8 ledger): GAP-J (delete Collection.Indexed), GAP-K (align Collection docs), GAP-L (hoist the four count-derived Array index witnesses onto Array.Protocol -- a NEW gap surfaced 2026-06-02, the ADT-family derive-for-free instance; net -21 lines, 0 witness_method on the index hot path, 173 array tests green) all LANDED on main (verified: clean trees, green builds; commits in §8). GAP-L's STEP-0 spike retired the SE-0335 hypothesis -- the loose Index associatedtype was the real blocker, fixed by `where Index == Index<Element>`. Critical stance (§7.1): evaluates the design, does NOT assume the current iteration/sequence/collection code is correct/complete. Open: GAP-A (Array/Stack equality-over-span -- next), GAP-B/C/E/F/H/I + the Deque/Set.Ordered witness-hoist generalization. documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-02): Tier 2 cross-package. A clean swift-linter build (fresh-main swift package update) fails in transitive dep swift-version-primitives with `ambiguous use of subscript(_:)` (Version.Semantic.Parser.swift:158, Version.Tools.Parser.swift:114). MECHANISM (verified 2026-06-02): the parsers' generic Input is constrained to `Collection.Slice.`Protocol` & Swift.Collection`, and BOTH vend a `subscript(bounds: Range<Index>)` requirement -- institute `-> Self`, stdlib `-> SubSequence` -- so `input[lo..<hi]` has two candidates at a generic call site. Institute Collection.Protocol refines Iterable, NOT Swift.Collection (orthogonal per the detachment DECISION), so a dual conformer carries two independent subscripts. ASYMMETRY: input-primitives' Input.Slice conforms to both yet is immune because it is CONCRETE (one witness satisfies both, SubSequence == Self); version-primitives breaks because its Parser is GENERIC over the dual constraint. The `& Swift.Collection` is a DEVIATION from the canonical Collection.Slice.Protocol-only parser input (parser-collection-protocol-migration.md While<Input: Collection.Slice.`Protocol`>), added to reach Swift.String(decoding:as:). BLAST RADIUS: 5 files / 3 packages (version Semantic/Tools/Calendar parsers, glob-primitives Glob.Pattern.Parser -- a DIRECT swift-linter dep, input-primitives = immune provider); only 2 errors surfaced (rest masked by cancellation + diagnostic batching). TIMELINE: dual constraint since 8fee211 (2026-05-19); regression tipped after 43d1fb7 (2026-05-26) by the June-1/02 single-root hierarchy refactor (3cc21cc/9f48e63/ae8efab) -- exact tipping commit unverified (bisect would require checking out historical upstream revisions, forbidden). OPEN QUESTION (verification gate, RES-027): Input.Element == Byte but String(decoding:as: UTF8) wants UInt8 -- a Byte-vs-UInt8 mismatch may sit BEHIND the ambiguity; a <=1h fix-and-build spike decides Option A (call-site disambiguation, tactical) vs Option B (drop & Swift.Collection + institute byte->text path, structural -- RECOMMENDED per RES-022). Options C (refine stdlib -- reverses detachment) + D (concrete input -- over-narrows) rejected. Report-not-fix: no source changed.

documents.topic: DECISION (v1.1.0, 2026-06-02; implemented in swift-collection-primitives 4042d2c, green on 6.3.2 + 6.5-dev): Tier 2 cross-package. swift-collection-primitives fails on Swift 6.5-dev (org.swift.64202605121a) at HEAD ad59898, green on 6.3.2 — all errors in Collection.Slice.Protocol's Range<Index> requirement (Collection.Slice.Protocol.swift:39) + its PartialRange default subscripts (+defaults.swift): `Self.Index does not conform to Escapable` / `'..<' on 'Comparable' requires Self.Index conform to Escapable`. ROOT CAUSE (verified 2026-06-02): the SE-0499 family relaxed Swift.Comparable/Equatable to ~Escapable on 6.4+, and Comparison.Protocol is a typealias to Swift.Comparable there (Comparison Primitives.docc:53), so the slice's `where Index: Swift.Comparable` NO LONGER transitively implies Escapable (compiler note: `'where Self: Escapable' is implicit here`), while Range<Bound> still requires Bound: Escapable and the base Collection.Protocol.Index is ~Escapable-admitting — a [HANDOFF-016] premise-staleness at the type-system level (the slice's own comment '(which also requires Escapable)' was true on 6.3.2, now false). EMPIRICAL (both toolchains, real package): unfixed → 6.3.2 GREEN / 6.5-dev FAIL; Candidate A (`where Index: Swift.Comparable & Swift.Escapable`) → GREEN on BOTH (applied, built, reverted; tree clean at ad59898). /tmp probe reproduces the exact 6.5-dev errors and shows Candidate B's storage obstruction identically on BOTH toolchains (`stored property of 'Escapable'-conforming struct has non-Escapable type 'NEIndex'` — a self-slice can't carry ~Escapable bounds). RECOMMENDATION = Option A: the literal execution of collection-index-escapable-consumer-fallout.md v1.3.0 DECISION ('Escapable is an operation-level requirement, escape-operations pin it'; its migration table names Collection.Slice.Protocol), forced into visibility by the toolchain — base keeps its ~Escapable-admitting Index, the slicing refinement pins Escapable ('base permits, refinement constrains'); non-breaking (0 ~Escapable-index conformers exist ecosystem-wide), evergreen, 1 token. Option B (admit ~Escapable via a non-Range API — the principal's disclosed lean) is FORECLOSED: a self-slice (SubSequence==Self) must encode its bound positions, so a ~Escapable index forces Self ~Escapable (cascading/degenerate) or an Escapable coordinate (contradicting the premise); B also reopens the closed v1.3.0 DECISION and fails [RES-018]'s structural test independent of its zero demand. CO-ARCHITECT FLAG: the disclosed B-lean contradicts the principal's OWN v1.3.0 DECISION ('~Escapable indices excluded from slicing by design'). C (concrete `where Index == Index<Element>` pin) over-constrains (drops custom Escapable index domains the v1.3.0 DECISION preserved); D (new ~Escapable-range primitive) + E (split the protocol) rejected. Report-not-fix: no source changed (Candidate-A edit applied for validation then reverted).

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-05): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. The first-principles, formally-grounded calculus answering 'which tower layer canonically owns a given variant/capability?' for the MSB tower (Memory -> Storage -> Buffer -> ADT) -- the THEORY layer generalizing the ad-hoc corrections (.Small->Memory; the store warts; the capacity axes) into one reusable framework, the science behind storage-memory-split.md + store-capability-elimination.md + Prism's capacity/end-state notes. ONTOLOGY: a data structure is a typed view over a store sigma : Location |-> Value (Reynolds); the four layers are the four mutually-orthogonal design decisions (Parnas secrets) -- Memory = byte-LOCATION + ALLOCATION (where bytes live, who frees them; the algebra of dom(sigma)); Storage = slot-TOPOLOGY + init-TRACKING (single- vs multi-region); Buffer = occupancy/access DISCIPLINE (count/order/dense-sparse); ADT = abstract CONTRACT. Span/Iteration/Algebra are orthogonal cross-cutting capabilities composed OVER cores; Index is a sibling axis. CALCULUS: project C onto the axis basis; DECOMPOSE any name bundling >=2 axes (the .Bounded=4-axes lesson); place each single-axis concern at min_sqsubset { L : owns(L,A) AND enforceable(L,inv(C)) } -- the lowest layer that owns the axis AND can enforce the invariant; express by distinct leaf types + composition + constrained extensions, NEVER an ad-hoc capability protocol/refinement (standing principal directive); stop at the honest hard floor. TWO SYMMETRIC FAILURE MODES: too-high (a concern parked above its axis -- a Parnas information-hiding violation; loses Reynolds representation independence; the .Small-at-Storage and Store.Creatable/Tracked-at-store mistakes; cured by the wart-triad inversion override->type-selection / universal-requirement->leaf-private / non-conformance->honest-absence) and too-low (a policy crushed into a leaf it cannot enforce -- a Dijkstra separation-of-concerns failure; the silently-overflowing Memory.Bounded leaf; the leak-record-below-the-allocation-owner; ~Escapable owners). FORMAL SEMANTICS [RES-024]: placement typing rules (P-Place / P-Decompose / P-Floor) + a soundness sketch (Lemma 1 No-Leak via Reynolds/Mitchell-Plotkin existential binding; Lemma 2 No-Crush via enforceable; Theorem: the judgment characterizes the unique placement committing neither failure mode = max-decomposition-bounded-by-correctness). CASE STUDIES validated BY the calculus (none contradicted): .Small/.Inline->Memory (storage-memory-split.md, DECISION); Store.Creatable/Store.Tracked->memory-leaf (store-capability-elimination.md + prism-wart receipts: leaf-private ledger Tracked.live=0 vs 5 leaked); .Bounded/.Fixed/.Static->4-axis bundle (capacity->Memory, growth->Memory-leaf, overflow-signal->Buffer-discipline FLOOR, typed-index->Index) per prism-capacity. PRIOR-ART SLR [RES-023] Kitchenham, parallel-subagent-verified [RES-020] 2026-06-05: Reynolds 1983 (repr. independence, paraphrased), Mitchell-Plotkin 1988 (ADT=existential, paraphrased), Wadler 1989, Liskov-Zilles 1974; Reynolds 2002 sep-logic (H:Address|->Value verbatim), Tofte-Talpin 1997 regions, RustBelt 2018 (DeallocSize = the leak-freedom floor anchor), Blazy-Leroy 2008 CompCert; Parnas 1972, Dijkstra EWD447 (SoC = his coinage), Wadler 1998 expression problem, van Wijngaarden 1965 orthogonality; cross-language Rust RawVec/SmallVec/ArrayVec/Allocator(nightly), C++ vector<T,Allocator>/pmr (P0619R3), Bonwick slab, Stepanov EoP, Strom-Yemini typestate, Haskell vector. Contextualization [RES-021]: the discipline-over-allocation split + inline-vs-heap-as-parameter are universal; the institute diverges deliberately via distinct leaf TYPES (compile-time type-selection -> 0-witness) over runtime allocator objects, and leaf-private static liveness. [MOD-*] REFACTOR BASIS (the deliverable for a later skill-lifecycle step, NOT edited here): new [MOD-PLACE] (lowest-correct-layer placement) + [MOD-PLACE-DECOMPOSE] + [MOD-PLACE-FLOOR] + [MOD-PLACE-AUDIT] (two-failure-mode lens) + [MOD-PLACE-EXPRESS] (decompose/compose not protocol); RESHAPE [MOD-009] (location satellites are Memory leaves composed by Storage, not Storage/Buffer satellites -- the canonical [MOD-*] rule that bakes a too-high placement); amend [MOD-012]/[MOD-035]; and EXTEND (do NOT override) sub-product-split-decision-rubric.md -- concern-placement is orthogonal to package-promotion (composition != package-split; Gate-1 brake intact). documents.displayName: The Storage/Memory Split — Un-fusing Storage<E>.Heap into Storage-discipline-over-Memory-leaf
documents.topic: DECISION (v1.1.1, 2026-06-04; seat-ratified v1.1.0 — all six asks; Phase E executed W-A…W-E, merge staged): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide — the Phase-D design packet for the follow-up #3 arc (dispatch HANDOFF-storage-memory-split.md, principal-authorized 2026-06-04; ratified v1.1.0). Un-fuses Storage<E>.Heap (one type fusing the Memory leaf — ManagedBuffer allocation/cleanup/CoW backing — and the Storage discipline) into Storage.Contiguous<M> (the #2-renamed Storage.Flat) over a NEW class-backed leaf Memory.Heap<E> in a NEW swift-memory-heap-primitives (per-action YES pending). Design = A3': insert Store.Tracked.Protocol (Store.Protocol + var initialization) between the FROZEN 4-op Store.Protocol and Storage.Protocol (which becomes a pure marker refinement — consumer-visible surface unchanged); relocate the ledger enum to store-primitives as Store.Initialization<E> ([MOD-032]-forced — the leaf needs it and must sit below storage); the cleanup oracle stays in the LEAF's Backing-class deinit (the bd04f32 wall: conditionally-Copyable generic structs cannot carry deinit — respected by construction, no stored ledger in the discipline, sidesteps follow-up #18); Contiguous gains conditional Copyable + the LAYERED conditional conformances (Store uncond / Tracked cond / Storage cond — the substrate-forwarding initialization witness un-parks the W3 A2-addendum item; the inert discipline witness is DELETED, making untracked substrates unrepresentable under dense disciplines). Source stability by typealias-first: Storage<E>.Heap = Contiguous<Memory.Heap<E>> + ledger alias + shim targets with @_exported re-exports — the ~230-file/25-package spelling surface incl. 209 S == Storage<E>.Heap pins compiles unchanged. THE THREE OPENS RESOLVED with /tmp probes (P1 /tmp/split-probe: 4 modules mirroring package boundaries, 21 checks ALL PASS debug+release, 0-witness_method in the concrete driver, SIL-level negative-copy receipt, C6 wall documented — unbound `extension Storage.Heap` via generic typealias REJECTED, pinned-Contiguous spelling is the migration form, zero consumer impact; P2 /tmp/split-probe-inline: composed Inline incl. the VALUE-GENERIC typealias Storage<E>.Inline<let n: Int> ALL PASS debug+release): (a) CoW chain Array→Linear→Contiguous→leaf typechecks through three conditional layers, diverges + double-frees nothing; (b) Inline's leaf = a tracked VALUE store (bitmap auto-track, consumer-drains, no class) — composition VIABLE but DEFERRED to follow-up #5 (the tracked-substrate semantics unification point, supervisor Q2); (c) typealias-first proven incl. pins + create + ensureUnique pinned surface. SPAN DESIGN BASELINE = POST-collapse single Span.Protocol (~Copyable, ~Escapable, borrow self) per seat relay 2026-06-04 — swift-span-primitives is the parallel Unify arc's zone, untouched here; Phase E branches off post-Unify mains. Six ask: items for the seat (A3' stack, conditional Storage.Protocol on Contiguous, package creation, converged-doc Memory-wording refinement, Inline deferral, CLCPM §3.4 fold-back) + the W-A…W-E wave plan (fresh .split-wt/ worktrees, per-step commits, suites + 0-witness receipts per wave, no push/tag).

documents.displayName: Eliminating Store.Creatable + Store.Tracked via Memory Decomposition
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-05; gated on the post-Cleave-4 Memory-leaf decomposition): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide — continues storage-memory-split.md. The wart-investigation verdict that BOTH store-tier capability protocols are ELIMINABLE against the memory-decomposed foundation (type-distinct Memory.Heap [ARC-allocating] / Memory.Inline [in-place] leaves under Storage.Contiguous<M>). Store.Creatable allocation -> the EXISTING Memory.Allocator.Protocol (it was a redundant store-tier duplicate); relocation -> where M: ContiguousMem (compile-time bulk-selection; overturns the prior irreducible). Store.Tracked -> a Memory.Heap-PRIVATE liveness record (the universal protocol is the wart; the leak-freedom kernel for ARC-backed ~Copyable memory is irreducible but leaf-private — proven: 5 leaked without it). The single-region Storage.Protocol marker dissolves. Keep only Store.Protocol 4-op + Span.Protocol. Principle: a store-tier capability protocol is a wart when its need is really a property of a specific memory leaf — decomposition converts runtime override->compile-time type-selection, universal stored requirement->leaf-private record, awkward non-conformance->honest absence. Revised Cleave-5 plan; flagged for post-Cleave-4 validation (Ring/Split range-set [still leaf-private], marker shape, CoW, real-leaf 0-witness SIL). Read-only investigation; receipts .probe-bank/prism-wart/.

documents.displayName: Occupancy Encoding II — Category Theory: Composition Over Refinement
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-08): Tier 3 ecosystem-wide. The category-theory angle of the occupancy-encoding problem: dissolve the Store.Sparse refinement by DECOMPOSITION + COMPOSITION (a product/tensor), not subtyping. MODEL: the sparse store is a PRODUCT Store ⊗ Occupancy of two ORTHOGONAL, NON-REFINING capability objects in the category 𝒱 of ~Copyable value types — a pure 4-op addressable Store (capacity/subscript/initialize/move) and an Occupancy algebra (allocate/free/isOccupied/occupied). Occupancy does NOT refine Store; a single concrete leaf satisfies both SEPARATELY. The generic buffer is parameterised over Store ONLY; allocation attaches by CAPABILITY CONJUNCTION in an extension constraint (where S: Occupancy) — the type-level shadow of the object product. CENTRAL PROOF (all on Swift 6.3.2, TOOLCHAINS=org.swift.632202605101a, [Verified 2026-06-08]; spikes /tmp/occ-cat 1–4 + cross-module): the prompt's forcing premise — bitmap-in-leaf ⟹ leaf owns allocation ⟹ surface richer than 4-op Store ⟹ FORCES the refinement — is FALSE. The richer surface lives on a SEPARATE orthogonal capability reached by conjunction/specialization, never by refining Store. Spike 2 compiles+runs a generic Slab<S: Store> whose allocation API is added in `extension Slab where S: Occupancy` — the 4-op Store.Protocol stays pure and unique. THE SHIPPING ARCHITECTURE ALREADY DOES THIS: Buffer.Arena reaches allocate/free via concrete `where S == Storage<E>.Arena` static calls (Buffer.Arena.Bounded ~Copyable.swift:57/77/91/121), Storage.Arena conforms only the 4-op Store.Protocol (Storage.Arena+Store.Protocol.swift:109); Storage.Split<Lanes,Elements> IS the tensor (two Store.Protocol factors; Copyable(Split) ⇔ Copyable(both), Storage.Split.swift:124). WARRANTED-REFINEMENT TEST (derive-for-free C1–C4) applied to 'should Occupancy refine Store': FAILS — dense leaves (Memory.Heap/Inline, Storage.Contiguous) are Store-only, sparse leaves are both ⟹ conformer sets OVERLAP not NEST ⟹ sibling + dual-conformance, NOT refine. Store.Sparse.Protocol should be a CONJUNCTION/marker inheriting BOTH (a protocol-lattice product), never a requirement-adding refinement of Store — sharpens (does not supersede) occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf.md's framing. TEARDOWN is a THEOREM of the composition: no generic buffer carries a deinit; teardown factors through the leaf's value-witness (move-only inline leaf deinit walks its own bitmap; heap leaf deinit on the refcounted class). Wall-1 (SE-0427) is AVOIDED by assembling S2 (uncond ~Copyable leaf + deinit) + S5 (cond-Copyable generic, no deinit), never S1. COPYABILITY = a product-preserving functor 𝖢: 𝒱→𝟐, 𝖢(A⊗B)=𝖢(A)∧𝖢(B), forced by SE-0427; ONE Slab<S> covers move-only inline-sparse AND conditionally-Copyable heap-sparse (Spike 3); the carve-out TYPE dissolves. CATEGORICAL STRUCTURES judged by [RES-021] (adoption≠necessity): product/tensor REAL+load-bearing; lens REAL (occupancy projection; get-put/put-get/put-put hold, Spike 4); free-monoid DEMOTED to a partial monoid action (alloc nondeterministic+partial; free∘alloc=id on occupancy); comonad-of-liveness REJECTED as decorative (no extend/duplicate consumer — Spike 3 builds the full buffer with zero comonadic structure). A–E ALL ACHIEVED on 6.3.2: (A) one Store.Protocol no refinement; (B) ≤1 bit/slot; (C) value semantics/cond-Copyable, carve-out types dissolved into one Buffer<S>; (D) self-cleaning teardown no buffer deinit Wall-1 avoided; (E) maximal decomposition + clean composition. CROSS-MODULE SIL: zero witness_method in the client driver of Slab<SparseHeapBacking<Int>>; sumLive<S: Store & Occupancy> specialises. THE ONE HONEST RESIDUAL (non-vacuous): the single copyability corner bit-density ∧ value-semantics ∧ inline — an inline bitmap-walking deinit forces move-only (SE-0427 Wall-1); the tombstone (Element?) form buys copyability by spending density; the trilemma admits any TWO never three. This is one corner's COPYABILITY, NOT a fat interface and NOT a type explosion — load-bearing (forces a leaf design choice, changes the result's copyability), traced to a fundamental language law with an open Evolution path (PITCH-0003). RESEARCH ONLY — no tower edits; if the seat adopts the conjunction reading the change is a protocol-shape note for the leaf packages, sequenced by the principal.

documents.displayName: Store.Inline: Span-Based Access vs the In-Place Raw Pointer
documents.topic: ACCEPTED (v1.1.0, 2026-06-09 — span-canonical-public, keep per-slot seam, no type unification, consumer-driven W3/W4): Tier 2 cross-package. Does the Span/~Escapable approach let Store.Inline's @_rawLayout inline storage be accessed move-safely WITHOUT raw pointers, replacing the in-place withUnsafePointer approach? Probe (Swift 6.3.2): a yielded MutableSpan/Span over @_rawLayout storage compiles, is move-safe (consume-move-then-reuse PASS debug+release), is genuinely lifetime-bound (escape rejected), and enforces exclusivity (overlap rejected) — so the move-dangle IS resolved as a safety property. BUT it does NOT make Store.Inline pointer-free: @_rawLayout typed access is irreducibly pointer-mediated on 6.3.2 (Swift.Span is built from an UnsafePointer); the internal withUnsafePointer remains. Unification (b): a uniform accessor surface via Span.Protocol/Span.Mutable.Protocol is attainable, but TYPE-level collapse of Store.Inline and Storage.Contiguous is blocked by the documented mutableSpan-property generic-forwarding wall AND occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf.md's placement law. RECOMMENDATION: adopt the additive Span accessors on Store.Inline (consumer-driven), conforming the capability protocols to match the heap path; do NOT pursue type-level unification; prefer yielding _read/_modify (no _overrideLifetime debt) where the access pattern allows. Flags explicitly that Store.Inline is committed (de84968 W3-pre) so this revisits committed code — recommendation only, needs principal authorization; no source edited.
documents.statusDetail: ACCEPTED 2026-06-09 (v1.1.0; was RECOMMENDATION v1.0.0). Principal ruling on ChatGPT+seat+research convergence: add Span/MutableSpan accessors to Store.Inline (additive, object-local) matching the heap Storage.Contiguous surface; Span = canonical public contiguous-region surface (raw-pointer coroutines internal); keep the per-slot typed seam public (region-view vs element-lifecycle are distinct concerns); NO type unification of Store.Inline/Storage.Contiguous; consumer-driven in W3/W4. Pointer-freedom is a surface invariant (leaf pointer irreducible on 6.3.2, auto-improves at SE-0465). Empirical probe Swift 6.3.2, scratch-only. Extends nonescapable-support-memory-storage-buffer.md (Candidate B / §4.2) + the 2026-06-02 depointer reflection. Implementation pending in the W3/W4 migration.

documents.displayName: Stdlib Array Family Source Archaeology (6.3.2 → 6.4.x → main)
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-10): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. Read-only multi-ref archaeology of swiftlang/swift (pinned: swift-6.3.2-RELEASE cd8d8ad0019, swift-6.4.x-DEVELOPMENT-SNAPSHOT-2026-06-07-a 0b7717dc5b6, main 6f5d855aedf; release/6.5 does NOT exist — main is the 6.5-dev trunk) answering the ratified tower's eight questions. Headlines: (Q2) stdlib Array/ContiguousArray/ArraySlice are Copyable-element-only on ALL refs (zero ~Copyable mentions, negative grep proof); the conditional-copyability precedent is InlineArray (EAGER copy, no CoW); SE-0527 explicitly considered-and-rejected a conditionally-copyable Array — retires the 'stdlib Array posture' phrasing in PROPOSAL-tower-perfected-design §0. (Q7) NO conditionally-Copyable CoW container exists anywhere (stdlib, swift-collections @ af174fe, Swift Evolution) — upstream's direction is sibling families (CoW-copyable vs Unique/Rigid move-only), convergent with the ratified two-column design; nearest artifact is swift-collections' staged-disabled Shared<Storage: ~Copyable> (unconditionally-Copyable CoW box, #if false, zero consumers), which validates the Shared mechanism; the façade stays shelved with SE-0527's runtime-copyability-dispatch objection named. (Q3) R-5 drain-box CONFIRMED stdlib-convergent: _ContiguousArrayStorage.deinit drains elements + _fixLifetime(self) (idiom to adopt in Box.deinit). (Q4) single-alloc template documented: allocWithTailElems_1 + projectTailElems + malloc-size capacity reclaim; ManagedBuffer admits Element: ~Copyable INCLUDING on 6.3.2 — the fusion door exists today (stays future/internal per ratification). (Q5) span-canonical validated; mutableSpan is a CoW boundary (_makeMutableAndUnique before vend); stdlib itself uses _overrideLifetime; lifetime spelling is @_lifetime on 6.3.2 → @lifetime on 6.4.x+ (pin until the gate bump). (Q6) InlineArray = Builtin.FixedArray managed aggregate (no deinit → conditional Copyable legal); the userland @_rawLayout analogue is unmanaged → Wall 1 — confirms occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf. (Q8) the R-6 miscompile shape is UNEXERCISED by stdlib (no guarding idiom to copy; no upstream issue exists). Trajectory: CoW/teardown stable 632→main; @unsafe wave, borrow/mutate accessor migration, SE-0499, SE-0527 merged-then-reverted (CMake) 2026-06-04 — UniqueArray/RigidArray re-land imminent. Errata: OutputSpan is SE-0485 (store-inline doc says SE-0527; se-0527-alignment doc says SE-0506 — both wrong).

documents.displayName: Hashed-Container Substrate Archaeology (stdlib · swift-collections · the sparse hashed leaf)
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-10): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. R1 of the post-archaeology arc — feeds the ADT-families executor's set/dict halt (GOAL-tower-adt-families.md:50-51, 'the hashing column discipline'). Verified findings: stdlib _DictionaryStorage/_SetStorage = ONE Builtin.allocWithTailElems_3/2 allocation [bitmap words | keys | values] whose class deinit walks ONLY occupied bits (POD-skipped, _fixLifetime close) — occupancy-lives-in-the-leaf shipped at industrial scale, byte-identical 632→main; linear probing FCFS, NO tombstones (backward-shift chain repair), 3/4 load factor, per-instance address seed re-seeded on resize, copy() preserves seed+scale (no rehash). swift-collections BasicContainers previews (UnstableHashedContainers trait, source-unstable, 6.4-gated) are the ONLY move-only hashed precedent: RigidSet/RigidDictionary = parallel allocations + struct deinit + Robin-Hood-default linear probing + bitmap, keys bound Hashable & ~Copyable via SE-0499 (Implemented Swift 6.4 — NOT on the 6.3.2 gate ⇒ institute Hash.Protocol stays the key bound until the bump). SE-0527 Future directions names the Rigid/Unique hashed family as the stdlib trajectory. Swiss-table: ZERO Swift precedent anywhere (stdlib FIXME names Robin Hood as the alternative). PRESCRIPTION: ordered family keeps dense-elements + POD position-index (Hash.Table re-spell at W5; evaluate backward-shift vs its tombstone sentinels); unordered move-only family REQUIRES the sparse hashed LEAF (single-allocation SoA bitmap+slots, Store.Protocol, leaf-owned drain) — Buffer.Slots/Store.Split is leak-by-construction for owned scattered elements (untracked-elements rule × Wall-1: S5 ADTs cannot deinit); hash policy lives in the family's table engine pinning the concrete leaf (Buffer.Arena pattern), never in the leaf or seam.

documents.displayName: Container-Protocol Lattice & Borrowing Iteration — the Audit-#5 Root Cause
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-10): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. R2 of the post-archaeology arc — root-causes Audit #5 (REPORT-W4:121, move-only-element columns lost Collection/Iterable membership) instead of record-and-accept. THREE-LAYER root cause: (1) conformance-bundling accident — the lattice protocols ALL admit Element: ~Copyable; the gate is Buffer.Linear's Span.Protocol CONFORMANCE bound S.Element: Copyable (bundled with the withUnsafeBufferPointer C-interop hatch) while the raw span accessor is unbounded — mechanically re-relaxable; (2) the suspected structural wall is NOT a wall — PROBE (6.3.2, debug+-O, preserved at .handoffs/probes-2026-06-10/r2-lattice-probe/): generic borrow-through-call reads via a value-{ get } subscript requirement over ~Copyable Element COMPILE AND RUN; consume-out (let v = t[0]) is compiler-rejected — so Collection.Protocol membership can extend to move-only-element columns, with element-RETURNING conveniences staying Copyable-gated (the SE-0516 restriction split); (3) upstream is mid-churn — SE-0516 Iterable in active review to June 18 with author-floated renames (post #25 SpanIterator), Container = future-directions draft only, lorentey: 'no project- or team-wide consensus'. The fork is false: re-relax AND the borrowing-iteration layer already exists (__IteratorChunkProtocol matches SE-0516's revised shape incl. Failure; institute divergence = typed maximumCount). DISCOVERY with arc-wide consequence: the whole lattice's ~Copyable associated types ride the EXPERIMENTAL SuppressedAssociatedTypes flag (bare 6.3.2 rejects the spelling) — gate-bump dossier item (SE-0503 spelling check). SE-0527's accepted shape (UniqueArray in principle, Swift module, span-first + BorrowingSequence only) matches the tower's post-fix posture.
documents.statusDetail: 12/12 claims [RES-020]-verified 2026-06-10 (one location correction: __IteratorChunkProtocol lives in swift-iterator-primitives); probe run twice debug+release. Research-only — the verified change-shapes belong to the executor/W5 owner. Watch: SE-0516 review closes 2026-06-18.

documents.displayName: Slot-Map Prior Art & the Generational Seam
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-10): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. R3 of the post-archaeology arc — settles the executor's halt item 'slot-map vs the deliberately-deferred Storage.Generational seam'. VERDICT: never conform the 4-op seam as-is — three independent reasons: (1) the seam's ledger is linear-prefix-shaped vs scattered generational occupancy (the R1 non-prefix-ledger argument, second application); (2) raw Index access bypasses generation validation — the leaf's entire purpose; prior art is unanimous (slotmap exposes NO positional access; generational-arena/thunderdome gate raw tiers as loud, occupancy-checked accessors returning the true handle); (3) the shipped reference (Buffer.Linked) drives the handle API exclusively (insert→Handle / validated subscript / remove; zero seam calls, grep-verified). The Handle API IS the generational seam; SlotMap composes as a PINNED-COLUMN family (Buffer.Arena occupancy-pin pattern; target naming Buffer.SlotMap per the DESIGN doc). Iteration: slot-scan now, occupied-region chunk iterator for future Iterable (s-c RigidSet precedent), dense-mirror consumer-evidence-gated, hop is DEAD upstream (HopSlotMap deprecated 1.1.0). Generation mechanics already exceed consensus (64-bit vs Rust's 32-bit + documented 2^31 wraparound). FLAGGED TO SEAT: Storage.Generational keeps per-slot state in two stdlib-Array side allocations (_generations/_occupied, verified :66/:70) — diverges from the occupancy law's single-allocation SoA Arena model (alignment candidate, not a blocker). Swift ecosystem census: ZERO slot maps anywhere (stdlib/s-c/SE/forums) — open field.
documents.statusDetail: 12/12 claims [RES-020]-verified 2026-06-10 (5 web-primary re-fetched, 7 local re-read; one wording precision folded in). Research-only; feeds the ADT-families executor's surface-first halt and the seat's ratification moment.

documents.displayName: Column Spelling Ergonomics — the Alias Vocabulary & the Gate Cost
documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-10): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. R4 of the post-archaeology arc, two halves. NEAR-TERM (W5-blocking): the column-alias vocabulary. Demand proven — test suites privately re-derive the same aliases (HeapColumn/SharedColumn/MoveArray, Array Surface Tests.swift:18-28) while the public surface ships zero; construction is already spell-free (split pinned constructors) — the gap is TYPE POSITION. Mechanism verdict (first-hand probes + stdlib precedent): generic typealiases on 6.3.2 are fully capable — generic params, ENFORCED constraints both forms, ~Copyable suppression, and namespace-enum-nested aliases with own params (the lawful shape under [API-NAME-001/002]; top-level compound NIO-style aliases are unlawful here; 'CoW' is never an identifier). Default generic args: dead (PR #591 closed 2017, no successor) AND SE-0527 argues against the pattern itself (viral type pollution, stack traces). Macros: closed named lists only (global_arbitrary_name). RECOMMEND Option A: a Column-style namespace vocabulary module (Array<Column.Shared<Int>>), Audit-#9-aligned; seat names it. LONG-TAIL: SE-0527's runtime-dispatch objection QUANTIFIED in the tower's shape — microprobe (-O, 6.3.2): always-unique per-element gate = 5.1 vs 0.8 ns/op ungated ⇒ ~4.3 ns worst case; 0 on move-only columns (static column choice voids objection #1); amortized to ~0 by the sanctioned span-first bulk factoring. Façade watch: s-c Shared unchanged (#if false @ af174fe); triggers recorded (un-gating commit or copyable-Box pitch).
documents.statusDetail: 6/6 web claims [RES-020]-verified; typealias capabilities + gate cost re-derived first-hand; probe artifacts preserved at .handoffs/probes-2026-06-10/r4-alias-gate-probe/. Naming is the seat's permanent-API call; two-column design unchanged.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-10): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. R5 of the post-archaeology arc — ring/bounded-queue prior art mapped onto the shipped Buffer.Ring + the queue family the executor landed mid-arc (validation + gap analysis). Findings: (1) layout consensus — s-c both generations use header{capacity,count,startSlot}+≤2 lazy segments; our Ring is the same family with runs carried in the typed initialization ledger; NIO CircularBuffer is the divergent ContiguousArray<Element?>+power-of-two-mask form (the tombstone trade, correctly not taken). (2) Teardown converges on ledger-driven two-region exactly-once at three owners — s-c CoW class deinit (_DequeBuffer IS a ManagedBuffer subclass: the ring-column FUSION TEMPLATE, completing the upstream single-allocation trio with _ContiguousArrayStorage and _DictionaryStorage), RigidDeque struct deinit→dispose(), our no-deinit-at-the-buffer + leaf oracle (strictest). (3) Bounded policy census {grow 1.5×/2×, trap, reject}: our rejection-first + typed capacityExceeded inverts s-c's trap-first — deliberate [API-ERR-001] divergence now documented. (4) Drop-oldest/overwrite ring semantics absent ECOSYSTEM-WIDE (verified searches) — open variant cell, consumer-gated. (5) Follow-ups: buffer-ring-consumer-api-boundary.md's DEFERRED trigger has FIRED (queue now composes Ring) — delta revisit due; queue _Package-Insights carries a stale pre-reshape nested-type claim to retire.
documents.statusDetail: 8/8 upstream claims [RES-020]-verified (permalinked at s-c af174fe + nio 7586225b); local claims spot-checked first-hand. Research-only; family already shipped — findings land as docs guidance + two seat flags.

documents.topic: RECOMMENDATION (v1.0.0, 2026-06-10): Tier 2 ecosystem-wide. The five riders of the post-archaeology arc, live-verified. (1) SE-0527 re-land watch: NOT re-landed — re-land PR swiftlang/swift#89698 is OPEN with CI churning 2026-06-10 (Windows smoke); UniqueArray/RigidArray dirs absent at main HEAD 81b9ead; institute shadow pre-check CLEAN (zero bare identifiers); re-check rides the merge. (2) R-6 filing dossier HELD (fresh YES required): draft issue skeleton assembled over the durable Experiments/cow-box-deinit-omission-miscompile repro (MinimalRepro + mitigation matrix incl. Q8 shapes 11-12 both-negative; drain-in-class-deinit = positive control; sibling-of-#86652 framing; no upstream duplicate exists). (3) Gate-bump 6.3.2→6.4 readiness: @_lifetime sweep = 194 files/436 occurrences; SuppressedAssociatedTypes → SE-0503 Accepted with a TWO-FLAG nuance (SuppressedAssociatedTypesWithDefaults Features.def:289 vs the older flag :464); SE-0499 key-bound swap; SE-0474 = Yielding accessors (Accepted, but impl partial behind CoroutineAccessors — verify 6.4-stable ships it); @unsafe wave hits 5 ManagedBuffer-consumer packages; SE-0516 closes 06-18. (4) Fusion scoping: the upstream single-allocation trio is complete (_ContiguousArrayStorage/_DictionaryStorage/_DequeBuffer); fused-box R-6 interaction hypothesis recorded UNVERIFIED with a mandatory ≤1h shape-13 spike recipe before any fusion design reliance; 2-alloc ratification + layer-collapse tension unchanged. (5) Consume-into-escaping-closure: the wall is SE-0390-NORMATIVE; SE-0528 Continuation is the sanctioned adjacent pattern (quotes our exact diagnostic); once-closures unpitched (SE-0073 Rejected 2016) — the W4 structured-consuming-sending ruling stands; watch = a FnOnce-style pitch.
documents.statusDetail: Sweep agent verified all four external watch items against primary sources (PR API, raw proposals, Features.def, forums) 2026-06-10; local counts + Experiments state re-derived first-hand. R-6 filing remains gated on a fresh principal YES.

narrativeSections.heading: Reflections
narrativeSections.body: Post-session reflections are captured via the **reflect-session** skill and processed via **reflections-processing**. They live under [Reflections/](Reflections/); see [Reflections/_index.md](Reflections/_index.md) for the date/title/packages/status table.

narrativeSections.heading: Moved to swift-memory-primitives/Research/
narrativeSections.body:
The following memory/pointer research has been consolidated into `swift-primitives/swift-memory-primitives/Research/`:

- `ordinal-cardinal-foundations.md` — Mathematical foundations for ordinal/cardinal separation
- `affine-scaling-operations.md` — Tier 3 research on scaling operations in affine spaces
- `pointer-architecture-comparison.md` — Swift stdlib pointer vs memory/pointer primitives
- `lifetime-dependent-borrowed-cursors.md` — Lifetime-dependent borrowed cursor patterns
